title: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@finova.com>, Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Hi Elena / Tom —

Following up with our weekly mutual action plan checkpoint for Finova's evaluation. High-level agenda for today's sync:

- Current evaluation status & key blockers
- SLA exception reported by Finova (details below)
- Remaining items for go/no-go decision and close timeline
- Action owners and target dates

Notes attached: Finova_MAP_v4.xlsx (updated timeline + evaluation checklist).

Quick status:
- Sandbox trial: complete (data ingest + embedding evaluation)
- API latency baseline: 120–160ms median on standard batch load (per our last run)
- Cost estimates: included in MAP v4 worksheet

SLA exception: Tom flagged intermittent 5xx spikes during peak test window yesterday (11/01). We need a quick triage and an exec-level acknowledgement of priority handling. Tom — can you paste the failure snippet in reply? Vivek, please own triage follow-up and ETA; Marissa and I will coordinate comms and any credit/SLA remediation asks with leadership.

Action items (initial):
- Vivek: collect logs and share root-candidate by EOD 11/03 — owner: Vivek Kulkarni
- Marissa: prepare impact note and draft request for temporary SLA exception / priority support — due 11/04
- Sean: stand by to escalate commercial remedies (service credit / uplift) if the root cause is infra-related — due 11/05
- Aisha: update MAP timeline with any slippage and send revised target close date — due 11/04

Please reply with availability for a 30-min bridge if needed.

Thanks,
Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Finova_MAP_v4.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

From: Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Elena Cruz <elena@finova.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 10:14:12 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Aisha — thanks for the note.

We've been hitting a cluster of 500/502 responses during our high-concurrency embedding runs. Sample below from yesterday at 15:23 PST: 

[ERROR] 2026-11-01T15:23:07Z - upstream fetch failed - status=502 - request_id=finova-req-3b9
Trace: upstream timeout after 1.2s, then 504 followed by 502 on retry

This spike impacted our pipeline for ~18 minutes and caused about 7% of the batch to fail and retry, which artificially inflated our token costs and blocked a subset of tests. Attaching a trimmed dump we pulled: error_dump_1103.txt.

Given this is a performance-sensitive evaluation and our go/no-go depends on predictable latency under load, we'd like Redwood to treat this as priority support. Elena and I are available for a bridge this afternoon if you can get an engineer on it.

Thanks,
Tom
Director of Platform Engineering
Finova Payments

[Attachment: error_dump_1103.txt]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 08:45:30 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Tom / Aisha —

Thanks for the logs, Tom. I'm kicking off triage now. Early observations: the 502s line up with a short-lived saturation event on the inference pool that correlates to a higher-than-expected prefix-cache churn during your burst tests. Hypothesis: a combination of cache thrash + an autoscaler throttle edge-case that delayed pod warmups under sudden concurrency.

What I'll do within the next 24 hours:
- Pull shard-level metrics and correlated pod lifecycle events (owner: Vivek)
- Replay the exact request pattern in a controlled staging environment to reproduce (owner: Vivek)
- Provide a mitigation playbook: short-term (rate-limit recommendation + temporary capacity bump) and medium-term (cache tuning + autoscaler config)

Immediate ask from Finova: could you share the exact request concurrency profile (RPS and batch sizes) and any synthetic test scripts so we can reproduce? If you can paste a small excerpt of the traffic pattern, that would speed up root cause.

Tentative ETA: preliminary triage findings by EOD 11/04; mitigation steps and timeline by 11/05. If we reproduce and confirm infra-related root cause, I'll recommend a temporary capacity uplift for your Dedicated eval window to stabilize latency while we push the autoscaler fix.

I'll be on a call at 11:30 — otherwise, available on Slack.

Thanks,
Vivek
Staff Engineer, Runtime Reliability
vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com

> On Tue, Nov 3, 2026 at 10:14 AM Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com> wrote:
> > Aisha — thanks for the note.
> > 
> > We've been hitting a cluster of 500/502 responses during our high-concurrency embedding runs....


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@finova.com>, Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 10:02:06 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Elena / Tom —

Appreciate the flag and Vivek's quick ownership. From a commercial/leadership perspective: if the root cause is on our side (infra/config), Redwood will classify this as an SLA incident for the evaluation window and make the following immediate commitments:

1) Priority support ticketing and 24x7 on-call coverage for the remainder of your evaluation burst window.
2) Temporary capacity uplift (no additional charge for the first 48 hours) to stabilize throughput while engineering deploys the autoscaler fix.
3) Contingent service credit conversation if the finalized P99 latency or error rate exceeds agreed thresholds for two consecutive evaluation days.

Marissa will draft the formal impact/credit letter and route for approval. Aisha — please add a short note in the MAP timeline documenting these commitments and adjust the close target if needed.

If you'd like an exec-level (VP/GM) check-in, I can arrange a 30-minute call tomorrow afternoon; otherwise we can run with Vivek's triage cadence.

—Sean
Head of Sales, Americas
sean_gallagher@redwood.com

> On Wed, Nov 4, 2026 at 08:45 AM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Tom / Aisha —
> > 
> > Thanks for the logs, Tom. I'm kicking off triage now. Early observations:...


From: Elena Cruz <elena@finova.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 17:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Thanks, Sean — that helps.

We accept the temporary capacity uplift and priority support immediately; please confirm start time and whether the uplift will be applied to our Dedicated evaluation pool or the shared staging pool. Tom and I are OK to defer an exec-level call unless the triage shows systemic infra gaps.

Updated action items (confirmed):
- Vivek: triage and mitigation plan — deliver by 11/05 EOD (confirmed)
- Marissa: draft impact/credit letter and send for approval — deliver by 11/05 EOD
- Aisha: update MAP timeline and send revised close recommendation — deliver by 11/06
- Sean: provision temporary capacity uplift for 48 hours starting 11/05 19:00 PST (pending ops confirmation)

We'll run an additional high-concurrency test during the uplift window and share results. Appreciate the quick response.

Best,
Elena Cruz
VP Product, Finova Payments

> On Thu, Nov 5, 2026 at 10:02 AM Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Elena / Tom —
> > 
> > Appreciate the flag and Vivek's quick ownership. From a commercial/leadership perspective:...

title: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Liz Carter <liz.carter@nimbus.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure

Hi Liz,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — appreciated the candid discussion about Nimbus's Q4 priorities and the bursty inference work you expect once the analytics pipeline goes into production. Per our conversation, attached is a proposed renewal amendment that moves your reserved pool from the current 8 GPUs to 16 GPUs and restructures the monthly commit to smooth the budget impact over the first quarter.

High-level proposal (attachment: redwood_16gpu_amendment_docusign.pdf):
- Baseline: preserve your existing baseline monthly commit for months 1–2 to avoid immediate billing shock.
- Ramp: months 3–6 scale incrementally (8 -> 12 -> 14 -> 16 GPUs) with prorated credits for partial-month activations.
- Billing: new monthly-commit rate effective at month 3; we suggest split invoices for the first 3 months (baseline portion + incremental capacity) to align with your internal chargeback cadence.
- Throughput guardrail: 95th percentile throughput guarantee at committed capacity; additional burst capacity available at defined overage rates.
- Onboarding credit: one-time 10% usage credit applied in month 1 to offset migration runbook work.

Why staggered activation? It reduces immediate cost impact while giving your SREs time to validate the new model variants and memory profiles. We’ll coordinate a two-week activation window for each incremental step and provide a rollback plan per activation.

Next steps I propose:
1) Confirm whether split invoicing (baseline + ramp) works for your procurement team.
2) If yes, I’ll prepare a DocuSign amendment and a short mutual action plan (MAP) with activation dates.
3) We can run a short readiness call (30 min) with your SREs and our runtime team to lock the activation windows.

I’ve attached a PO stub you can hand to procurement to speed approvals (Nimbus_PO_Q4_2026.pdf). If you prefer, I can include your procurement contact directly on the DocuSign routing.

Would Thursday or Friday next week work for a 30-min readiness call?

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0114

---

From: Liz Carter <liz.carter@nimbus.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procure@nimbus.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure

Thanks, Karthik.

This looks promising — a few quick questions from procurement and our platform lead before we put together a PO: 
- Can you confirm whether you can deliver split invoices (we prefer separate line items for baseline vs. ramp)?
- SLA: what is the 99th percentile latency SLA tied to the committed pool? Are credits automatic?
- Security evidence: procurement needs the SOC 2 evidence package. Do you have a shared folder or link?
- DocuSign routing: can the amendment route to Procure (procure@nimbus.ai) first, then to me?

I’ve looped in procurement on this thread. Their lead is Patty Gomez (procure@nimbus.ai). Patty — we’re aiming to have this live in early Nov if we can get terms and PO in place.

Thanks — Liz
Liz Carter
Head of Platform, Nimbus Analytics
liz.carter@nimbus.ai

---

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure

Folks — forwarding Liz's procurement questions. Quick internal sync before I reply to Patty/Liz:

Quoted message:
> From: Liz Carter <liz.carter@nimbus.ai>
> To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
> Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 10:04:00 -0700
> Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure
>
> (see above)

My suggested positions:
1) Billing: we can produce split invoices (one line item for baseline, one for incremental ramp) for the first 3 months. Finance will need the billing codes; recommend we request Nimbus's chargeback GLs in the PO.
2) SLA: propose to commit to a 99th percentile token latency SLO at committed capacity; SLA credits automatic via monthly credit schedule (cap at 30% monthly invoice). Need legal to sign off on the precise credit formula.
3) Security: send the SOC 2 package link (we have the redacted evidence bundle); Mina can attach the pointer.
4) Discount flexibility: we can offer a one-time 5% promotional discount off the incremental commit if they sign before Nov 15. This preserves list pricing while rewarding quick closure.

Operational notes for activation windows: staggered steps will be executed with an ops playbook (two-week windows, production validation checkpoint, automatic rollback trigger if top-line errors exceed 2%). We should offer a single-day maintenance window per activation step.

Marissa — can you confirm margin impact for the 5% promo and whether we need AE signoff to approve? Mina — can you confirm SOC2 link and any additional evidence procurement always requests?

Thanks,
Karthik

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Renewal proposal: staggered 8→16 GPU upgrade & commit restructure

Karthik — confirmed, we can accommodate the 5% limited-time promo on the incremental portion. Margin impact is acceptable for a strategic expansion; finance approves with AE/Director signoff.

Mina — please drop the SOC2 package link into the reply (redacted bundle) and include a note about our standard data retention and audit log exports. Also flag any items that typically trigger procurement follow-ups (e.g., encryption-at-rest confirmations, AICPA attestation details).

Once Mina posts the links, I suggest we: 1) accept Liz's routing for DocuSign (procure -> Liz), 2) include the split-invoice language in the amendment, and 3) send a calendar invite for the readiness call (I can host post-signature).

Marissa
VP, Customer Success
Marissa Cole
marissa.cole@redwood.com

---

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Patty Gomez <procure@nimbus.ai>
Date: Mon, 26 Oct 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: SOC2 / evidence bundle & answers for Nimbus procurement

Patty — attaching the pointer to our SOC 2 Type II package (redacted evidence bundle): https://drive.redwood.com/soc2/redacted_bundle_2026 (attachment stub).
Key notes:
- Encryption: all customer data at rest is encrypted with KMS-managed keys; we provide HSM-backed key rotation for Dedicated/private customers upon request.
- Audit logs: per-route request traces and token-level metering available; retention configured via Console (we default to 90 days for hosted customers; Dedicated can opt for longer retention).
- SLA/Credits: SLA crediting is automatic and applied monthly; legal will provide the formula in the amendment.

If you need any extracted artifacts for legal review (system architecture diagram, control matrices), ping me and I’ll expedite.

Thanks,
Mina Haddad
Security & Compliance
mina.haddad@redwood.com

---

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Liz Carter <liz.carter@nimbus.ai>, Patty Gomez <procure@nimbus.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Final amendment & DocuSign link — 8→16 GPU renewal (staggered activation)

Hi Liz / Patty —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Per our internal approvals and Mina's SOC2 pointer, attached is the final amendment and the DocuSign envelope for signature: https://docusign.net/embedded/abcdef123456 (attachment stub redwood_16gpu_amendment_docusign.pdf).

Summary of final terms (high level):
- Capacity: reserved pool expands from 8 GPUs to 16 GPUs.
- Activation schedule: 2-week windows over months 3–6; each activation step is pre-coordinated with Nimbus SREs.
- Billing: split invoices for months 1–3 (baseline + incremental ramp). Full consolidated monthly invoice begins month 4.
- Pricing: incremental commit priced as shown in the amendment; one-time 5% promotional credit applied to the incremental commit if signed before 2026-11-15.
- SLA: 99th percentile token latency SLO for committed capacity; automatic monthly crediting for SLA breaches (details in amendment).
- Security: SOC 2 Type II evidence pointer included; Dedicated customers can request HSM/KMS integration options.

Patty — please confirm the PO number and preferred invoice remit details so we can populate the DocuSign fields. If you want the envelope routed: Procure (procure@nimbus.ai) -> Liz (liz.carter@nimbus.ai) -> finance@redwood.com, we can set that order.

If everything looks good, please sign the DocuSign and we’ll schedule the readiness call (I’ll propose times after we see the signed amendment).

Thanks — excited to get Nimbus scaled up.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0114

Attachments referenced: Nimbus_PO_Q4_2026.pdf, redwood_16gpu_amendment_docusign.pdf

title: Quarter-end 48hr: commit validation & daily rollup
Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Taylor <alex.taylor@brightcommerce.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up

Alex — great to connect today. Quick recap of what we heard and a few concrete asks so we can size a dedicated pool and propose a recommended GPU mix: 

Summary of requirements from call:
- Preferred path: Dedicated capacity (you indicated a desire for isolated, predictable throughput; potential VPC transition later).
- Typical steady TPS: ~120 requests/sec.
- Peak burst TPS: ~420 requests/sec sustained for ~10–60 minutes; single-day spike up to ~700 rps for ~30 minutes during marketing events.
- Average token mix (input + output): ~850 tokens; typical output ~300 tokens.
- Latency target: P95 < 250ms for generation tokens, overall end-to-end target ~400–500ms if possible.
- Functional needs: KV cache for dialogue context, streaming responses, and occasional tool/function calls.

Items attached: bright_capacity_workbook_v1.xlsx (worksheet for you to confirm traffic profiles + token distributions), SOC2 scope summary we discussed.

Questions / asks (please reply inline):
1) Confirm the steady / peak TPS numbers above and typical concurrency per user session (how many concurrent browser sessions expected during peak?).
2) Are you planning to pin a single model family (e.g., Llama-derivative, MPT) or expect multiple variants in production?
3) Any hard constraints on GPU types (A100 vs H100) or explicit procurement restrictions?
4) Do you need on-prem/VPC in the first 90 days, or is cloud Dedicated with a VPC later acceptable?

Next steps: once we have your confirmed numbers I’ll loop in Marissa to provide an initial SKU/concurrency recommendation and Sean for any special SLA/contract asks. We can also run a short benchmark against a small reserved pool if you’d like a proof point.

Thanks — talk soon.

--
Karthik Iyer
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com
(415) 555-0129

Attachment: bright_capacity_workbook_v1.xlsx
Attachment: soc2_scope_summary.pdf

From: Alex Taylor <alex.taylor@brightcommerce.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T16:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up

Karthik — thanks for the recap. Confirming numbers and adding a few clarifications: 

- Steady TPS: 110–130 rps (120 is a good mid).
- Peak sustained: ~420 rps for product catalog updates; daily marketing spike can reach ~650–720 rps for ~20–40 minutes.
- Concurrent browser sessions: expect ~2.5k active users, but only ~6–8% will be issuing generation requests during peak (so ~150–200 concurrent requesting sessions).
- Token mix: median total ~720 tokens, mean closer to 850 (heavy tails for long-form summaries). Output average ~280–320 tokens depending on prompt.
- Model plan: start with a single production model (we currently run a Llama-variant fine-tuned for retrieval). We may add a smaller reranker/embedding model later.
- GPU preference: our infra team prefers H100 for latency-sensitive workloads but will consider A100 if cost delta is material.
- VPC/on-prem: want VPC-first; on-prem only if we mandate for compliance later — initial deployment should be Dedicated in a customer VPC.

Also: procurement will want a ballpark monthly committed cost to compare to alternatives — high-level estimate is fine for now.

Regards,
Alex Taylor
Director, Platform Engineering — BrightCommerce
alex.taylor@brightcommerce.com

> > Alex — great to connect today. Quick recap of what we heard and a few concrete asks so we can size a dedicated pool and propose a recommended GPU mix: 
> > (see attached workbook)
>
Attachment: procurement@brightcommerce.com included on request

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T09:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up

Team — forwarding Alex's answers from BrightCommerce (below). Important sizing notes: 
- Steady ~120 rps, peaks ~650–720 rps for ~20–40 minutes.
- ~150–200 concurrent requesters during peak.
- Token mean ~850, median ~720; output ~280–320.
- They want Dedicated in a customer VPC initially; prefer H100 if latency justifies cost.

Marissa — can you: 
1) Propose an initial SKU mix (node counts + GPU types) and a concurrency plan (num of workers / max concurrency per GPU) that hits P95 <250ms generation tokens for their profile? Include quick notes about caching (prefix/KV) and whether quantization (8-bit) is a safe win here.
2) Give a back-of-envelope committed monthly estimate we can share with procurement (ballpark).

Sean — flagging for potential SLA/contract language (we may need a committed throughput SLA and planned burst allowances).

I attached the Bright workbook they filled; please edit and return. If you want, we can propose a small benchmark (1–2 node reserved pool) to validate latency assumptions before full commitment.

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Alex Taylor <alex.taylor@brightcommerce.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T16:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up

(see above)

Attachment: bright_capacity_workbook_v1.xlsx

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T11:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Decision grid for Dedicated capacity: GPU mix & TPS follow-up

Thanks for the context — I ran the numbers off the workbook and sketched a pragmatic starting blueprint. High-level recommendation for BrightCommerce: 

Recommendation (initial reserved pool):
- GPU type: H100 80GB preferred for latency; A100 80GB as backup if procurement pushes on cost.
- Node count: start with 4 H100 nodes (NVIDIA H100 80GB) in a dedicated pool.
- Concurrency per GPU: target 8–12 simultaneous generation streams per GPU for long-form workloads (850 tokens avg). For shorter requests we can ramp concurrency higher.
- Autoscaling: configure burst buffer to add 2 additional nodes for short spikes (health & warm-up to avoid cold KV misses).
- Caching: enable prefix/KV caching for dialogues — expect ~20–30% token reduction on average for sessioned users, which reduces effective token throughput.
- Quantization: recommend performance-validated 8-bit quantization for the primary model to recover token throughput while maintaining quality; we will validate on a small benchmark.

Why 4 nodes? Conservative math: at ~850 tokens/request and average generation speed on H100, 1 H100 optimized with our runtime (quantized path) can sustain roughly ~100–160 rps depending on prompt shape. With 4 nodes we cover steady-state 120 rps and provide headroom for 420–650 rps with short bursts when combined with autoscaling/burst capacity and caching. If they want guaranteed 700 rps sustained for 30 minutes we’d recommend increasing to 6 nodes or pre-warm 2 additional nodes during campaign windows.

Ballpark committed cost (monthly) — rough estimate for commercial discussion only:

Thanks for the context — I ran the numbers off the workbook and sketched a pragmatic starting blueprint. High-level recommendation for BrightCommerce: 

Recommendation (initial reserved pool):
- GPU type: H100 80GB preferred for latency; A100 80GB as backup if procurement pushes on cost.
- Node count: start with 4 H100 nodes (NVIDIA H100 80GB) in a dedicated pool.
- Concurrency per GPU: target 8–12 simultaneous generation streams per GPU for long-form workloads (850 tokens avg). For shorter requests we can ramp concurrency higher.
- Autoscaling: configure burst buffer to add 2 additional nodes for short spikes (health & warm-up to avoid cold KV misses).
- Caching: enable prefix/KV caching for dialogues — expect ~20–30% token reduction on average for sessioned users, which reduces effective token throughput.
- Quantization: recommend performance-validated 8-bit quantization for the primary model to recover token throughput while maintaining quality; we will validate on a small benchmark.

Why 4 nodes? Conservative math: at ~850 tokens/request and average generation speed on H100, 1 H100 optimized with our runtime (quantized path) can sustain roughly ~100–160 rps depending on prompt shape. With 4 nodes we cover steady-state 120 rps and provide headroom for 420–650 rps with short bursts when combined with autoscaling/burst capacity and caching. If they want guaranteed 700 rps sustained for 30 minutes we’d recommend increasing to 6 nodes or pre-warm 2 additional nodes during campaign windows.

Ballpark committed cost (monthly) — rough estimate for commercial discussion only: 
- 4x H100 reserved monthly: ~$[redwood_estimate_placeholder]k/month (will provide exact pro forma once SKU & region confirmed).

title: Prod Impact: AZ capacity exhaustion — routing play & exec brief
preview:
From: Tim Dow <tim.dow@hyperapps.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@acme.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:23:00Z
Subject: urgent: sharp latency + 5xx spike in us-east-1 (AZ2)

Hannah —

We started seeing a major latency increase and a burst of 5xx errors for our production traffic in us-east-1 around 08:15 UTC. P50/P95 both jumped and end-users on our east coast clusters are timing out on inference calls. Our SRE has traced it to an unusual queueing pattern to the Redwood endpoint (tokenization appears fine). Can you confirm if anything on your side is saturated? We need a routing/failover now — can you shift traffic to another region or an alternate model variant? This is customer-impacting at scale.

I can provide logs if that helps. Please escalate — we have our execs watching.

—Tim
Tim Dow
Head of Platform, HyperApps


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Tim Dow <tim.dow@hyperapps.com>, Elaine Park <elaine@acme.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:28:00Z
Subject: Re: urgent: sharp latency + 5xx spike in us-east-1 (AZ2)

Tim — thanks for the heads up and sorry for the impact. Looping in SRE and ops to triage immediately.

Immediate plan (short):
- Markus/Sean: confirm the health of the GPU pools and whether autoscale failed to clear backpressure.
- If pool is exhausted, execute temporary routing override to steer new requests to us-west-2 and enable degraded-model fallback for HyperApps until capacity is restored.
- Marissa/CS: notify the HyperApps customer success contact with an ETA and offer a mitigation credit if SLA is violated.

I will send an executive note once we have confirmation and the routing change is applied.

Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:36:00Z
Subject: Re: urgent: sharp latency + 5xx spike in us-east-1 (AZ2) — triage update

Hannah / team — triage update:
- Observed: AZ2 GPU pool in us-east-1 shows sustained enqueue depth and GPU utilization at ~99% for the last 12 minutes. Memory pressure caused a degradation path where small-batch async workers queued up. Autoscaler attempted to add capacity but hit soft quota check and did not spin new nodes (we're hitting a cloud account-specific limit).
- Impact: New requests queue => p95/p99 latency spike + transient 5xx when queues overflow.

Mitigation I recommend now:
1) Flip Smart Routing priority for HyperApps to prefer us-west-2 (live routing override) — low risk and reversible.
2) Apply degraded-model policy for this tenant (runtime quantized variant) to lower per-request GPU load while preserving correctness.
3) Kick off quota exception with infra (post-mortem followup).

I've uploaded a concise runbook for the cutover and rollback steps.
[Attachment: cr-cutover-playbook.pdf (PDF)]

I'll be standing by for the routing override.

Markus
Markus Klein
SRE Lead, Inference Infrastructure


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:45:00Z
Subject: Re: urgent: sharp latency + 5xx spike in us-east-1 (AZ2) — routing override applied

All — applied temporary routing override for HyperApps to prefer us-west-2 at 08:44 UTC. Also enabled tenant-level degraded-model fallback. Early telemetry:
- New connection success rate for HyperApps rose from 86% -> 99% for new sessions within 90s.
- P95 latency trending down (from ~1.6s to ~350ms for routed requests).

I captured the override event and the relevant logs: temp-routing-override.log (attached). Will monitor for any leftover failbacks and warm up caches in the target region.

Hannah / team — triage update:
- Observed: AZ2 GPU pool in us-east-1 shows sustained enqueue depth and GPU utilization at ~99% for the last 12 minutes. Memory pressure caused a degradation path where small-batch async workers queued up. Autoscaler attempted to add capacity but hit soft quota check and did not spin new nodes (we're hitting a cloud account-specific limit).
- Impact: New requests queue => p95/p99 latency spike + transient 5xx when queues overflow.

Mitigation I recommend now:
1) Flip Smart Routing priority for HyperApps to prefer us-west-2 (live routing override) — low risk and reversible.
2) Apply degraded-model policy for this tenant (runtime quantized variant) to lower per-request GPU load while preserving correctness.
3) Kick off quota exception with infra (post-mortem followup).

title: Scope review: combined BAA/FERPA addendum for Starlight / Ridgewood pilot
preview:
From: Legal Ops (Starlight) <legal@starlighthealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Kramer <sara@legalops.com>, IT Admin <itadmin@ridgewood-k12.edu>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 09:13:00 -0700
Subject: Request: combined BAA + FERPA addendum for pilot

Hi Irene,

Thanks for taking this on. Starlight Health is kicking off a 6-week pilot with Ridgewood K-12 to surface de-identified EHR snippets from school-based clinics into a clinical-assist workflow. Ridgewood has asked that we provide a combined Data Processing Addendum that addresses both HIPAA (BAA) and FERPA constraints for student health data.

High-level asks:
- Can Redwood accept a combined addendum that covers both regulated populations for this pilot?
- What subprocessors will be involved (hosting, telemetry, analytics)?
- Any constraints on cross-border transfer for backups/analytics?
- Timeline: they want a draft addendum + redline window inside 10 business days.

I attached a dataflow diagram and a CSV of the EHR schema the pilot will use. Please confirm next steps and whether Redwood needs any additional vendor evidence (SOC2, KMS cert).

Thanks,
Mark Hernandez
Sr. Counsel, Starlight Health
legal@starlighthealth.com

Attachments: starlight_dataflow.pdf, ridgewood_ehr-schema.csv


From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
To: Mark Hernandez <legal@starlighthealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: combined BAA + FERPA addendum for pilot

Mark —

Appreciate the clear list and attachments. Quick responses up front: we can scope a combined addendum for a narrow pilot, but there are a few design choices that materially affect subprocessors and transfer obligations.

What we need from you to make a clean draft:
- Clarify whether Ridgewood requires student-identifiers to be persistently stored (student_id, DOB) or whether linkage will be via short-lived tokens.
- Expected residency for backups and analytics (US-only vs EU/US hybrid).
- Whether Ridgewood requires KMS integration (customer-managed keys) or will accept Redwood-managed keys with audit logs.

Next steps from our side: I’ll draft a proposed-addendum-outline.docx that maps clauses to HIPAA/FERPA controls and lists subprocessors. Marissa (privacy) will validate data classification and Ben (in infra) will confirm KMS/backup residency options. Can we schedule a 45-min technical workshop tomorrow (Tues) 10–10:45am PT?

If that works I’ll send a calendar invite and a first-pass draft for redlines.

Best,
Irene Choi
Director, Partner Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference
irene_choi@redwood.com

Attachment: proposed-addendum-outline.docx


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 14:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: notes on combined BAA/FERPA scope (do not forward outside).

Team —

I reviewed the dataflow and schema. Key points to include in the draft:
- If Ridgewood requires persistent identifiers, FERPA will apply and we need explicit language about student education records and the limited purpose. A combined addendum is feasible but must call out permitted processing for each category (PHI vs education records) and who is the data controller for each.
- Subprocessors we will likely list: cloud-hosting (Redwood-managed GCE/GKE equivalent), metrics vendor (TelemetryCo), and model weights repository. For any analytics that leave the primary tenant scope, we should add an opt-in clause.
- Residency: Redwood's primary serving region can be US-only; backups and analytics currently may flow to our EU/US analytics cluster in Dublin unless we configure a US-only retention for this customer. That setting is a per-customer toggle but requires committed capacity changes if they want guaranteed US-only backups (we'll need infra signoff).
- KMS: We support customer-managed keys with HSM-backed KMS. If Ridgewood requests CMK, there will be a small operational onboarding cost and a change to the SLA on key-rotation windows.

I’ll annotate the proposed outline with these choices. Ben, can you confirm whether reserved capacity + US-only backup can be guaranteed within the pilot timeline or whether we need a Dedicated pool?

Marissa
Senior Privacy Counsel
Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Internal: notes on combined BAA/FERPA scope (do not forward outside).

Marissa —

Short answer on infra: for a 6-week pilot we can enable US-only serving and backups in our hosted environment with a temporary residency flag. That will not require a Dedicated pool, but it does require us to pin capacity and enable the US-only analytics path which takes ~48 hours for config + validation. Customer-managed KMS is possible but onboarding will take ~5 business days (HSM provisioning + policy review).

Operational implications to flag in the draft:

Subject: Quick recap + proposed 3-week pilot runbook (next steps)

Hi Anna — thanks again for the call earlier. Short recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth.

Recap (what we heard):
- Goal: validate Redwood inference for a customer-facing triage assistant (60-80% intent coverage, <150ms p95 on typical queries).
- Key success criteria: accuracy on the three priority intents, latency target, and integration with Novus SSO and audit logs.
- Constraints: sample data is redacted, infra team will provide a staging VM with access rules.

Proposed Pilot (3 weeks):
1) Week 0 (this week) — environment setup, access + baseline perf run
2) Week 1 — model/probe validation against sample prompts, iterate prompt templates
3) Week 2 — end-to-end integration test + acceptance run vs. success criteria

Owners (proposed):
- Redwood technical lead: Jordan Blake (me) — main contact, model/config changes
- Redwood CS / onboarding: Monica Patel — test orchestration, acceptance checklist
- Novus infra: Rajiv Sen — staging access and log exports

Immediate asks from us:
- Can Rajiv share a staging VM hostname and an SSH user for the test harness by Friday?
- Anna — confirm the three priority intents in the attached sheet and any additional acceptance KPIs.

I attached a draft runbook (redwood-pilot-runbook-v1.pdf) and a success-criteria spreadsheet. If that looks good I’ll schedule a 30-min kickoff for Thursday.

Thanks — Jordan

title: Renewal upsell: Dedicated pool expansion — verification + operational handoff
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Dan Murray <dan.murray@oliveai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal upsell: Dedicated pool expansion — scheduling verification test

Hi Dan,

Congrats again on the renewal — excited to continue supporting Olive AI. Per the renewal paperwork we added +8 reserved GPUs to your Dedicated pool (going from 12 -> 20 A100-equivalent units), bumped baseline throughput to the "Sustained 24/7" SLA, and added burst capacity for traffic spikes. Before we mark the expansion complete we need to validate the configuration with a short set of acceptance tests and then do an ops handoff.

Proposed quick plan:
- Wed 2026-10-01 14:00 UTC: coordinated load run (our harness + your traffic replay)
- Targets: sustain 200k tokens/min with p95 token latency <= 140ms at 32-token responses, error rate < 0.5%
- Tests: steady-state 30m, 5x scaled ramp to simulate peak, KV cache warm and cold runs
- Deliverables: CSV of raw telemetry, summary report, ops runbook for on-call, signed acceptance email

I've attached a proposed test plan (rwd_proposed_test_plan_20261001.xlsx). Can you confirm Dan + infra availability? If you prefer a different window we can adjust.

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

-----
Attachment: rwd_proposed_test_plan_20261001.xlsx (spreadsheet; test schedule, metrics list)


From: Dan Murray <dan.murray@oliveai.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <contracts@oliveai.com>, Olive AI - Infra Team <infra@oliveai.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal upsell: Dedicated pool expansion — scheduling verification test

Thanks Karthik — Wed 14:00 UTC works for us. I'll have Infra on the call and we'll run a traffic replay from our staging traces. A couple clarifications:

1) When you say "200k tokens/min", is that measured at the Redwood ingress (post-routing) or at the model process (tokens produced)? We need to map to our billing meter.
2) Can you include a p99 latency snapshot in the report? Our internal SLOs are tight and p99 regressions would block sign-off.

I'll make sure contracts@ is looped for any final procurement notes after acceptance.

—Dan
Senior Infra Eng, Olive AI
dan.murray@oliveai.com



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Dan Murray <dan.murray@oliveai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 17:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal upsell: Dedicated pool expansion — scheduled load run results (summary)

Dan —

Thanks for running with us earlier. Summary from today's coordinated run (attached: olive_load_test_summary_20261001.csv). Highlights:

- Configuration validated: Dedicated pool increased to 20 A100-equivalents, model pinned to rwd-gpt-3.5-xded (int8 quantized variant)
- Steady-state (30m): average throughput 205,400 tokens/min; p95 latency = 128ms; p99 latency = 312ms
- Error rate: 0.22% (mostly transient socket timeouts during one 30s network blip)
- Burst ramp (5x): system sustained 920k tokens/min for 60s ramps without queue overflows; tail latencies increased to p99=680ms during bursts
- Cache behavior: warm-KV runs saw 9% lower per-token CPU on model hosts compared to cold runs

Deviations vs proposed acceptance: p95 < 140ms met; p99 is higher than you'd prefer (312ms steady, 680ms during burst). Our SRE team investigated and identified two mitigations we recommend before formal sign-off:
- Increase GPU worker prefetch concurrency by 12% (low risk, config change)
- Adjust instance-level network MTU on one affected AZ (fixes observed socket fragmentation)

Subject: VPC dry-run: ops coordination, KV-cache warmup and fallback plan

Morning all —

We have the VPC cutover dry run window today at 13:00 PT for the customer-facing cluster(s). Goal for this dry run is to:
- validate network ACLs and NAT/Gateway routing for the private nodes
- execute KV-cache warmup (short generator workload) and capture warmup logs
- run the basic smoke suite (connectivity, auth, inference hello, telemetry ingestion)
- exercise the fallback: route to hosted model variant if backend pool fails

Notes / asks:
1) EdgeCloud: please confirm the BGP session reset step (step 7 in the checklist) and whether there will be a transient route flap longer than 10s. We need that to coordinate the warmup pacing.
2) Sean / Marcus: I'll need a pair to watch KV-cache hit rates and to trigger the manual fallback if we see repeated 503s.
3) Mina: can you run the smoke scripts and post logs to the Drive link below (short retention acceptable).

Attachments: redwood-vpc-dryrun-checklist-v1.pdf (checklist), I will push KVCACHE warmup plan to the Drive.

Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.company/drive/folders/ops/vpc-dryrun-2026-10-02
Jira: INF-4821

Plan for timing (PT):
- 12:45: ops bridge (quick sync)
- 13:00–13:10: network reset / route validation (EdgeCloud lead)
- 13:10–13:25: KV-cache warmup + telemetry sanity
- 13:25–13:40: smoke tests (auth, simple inference, telemetry)
- 13:40–13:55: fallback exercise and validation
- 14:00: short readout and next steps

If anything changes, call the bridge.

Thanks —
Irene

Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-10-02T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Post-dryrun readout: observations + artifacts

Summary from the 13:00 PT dry run (short readout):
- Network: EdgeCloud performed BGP reset at 13:03 — observed transient route convergence ~9s. No packet loss >1% post-convergence.
- KV-cache warmup: followed Irene's recommendation: 10 minutes single-stream priming then a 60s 40-concurrency spike. Result: steady-state hit ratio reached 78% after ~6 minutes of priming; spike caused temporary batch queue increase but no 5xx. Logs attached.
- Smoke tests: auth, simple-infer, telemetry ingestion all passed. One telemetry probe lag (~11s) during route convergence but recovered.
- Fallback exercise: manual fallback to hosted-model variant was exercised at 13:35 by Sean — traffic shift confirmed and validation traces show successful failover within 18s.

Action items / follow-ups:
1) Update the checklist to include a note that route convergence may be up to 12s on some peers (EdgeCloud will add).
2) Add a short automated probe that detects KV hit ratio falling below 50% for >30s and raises an alert tied to automatic fallback (INF-4821).
3) Archive warmup logs to the Drive folder and attach a 1-page summary to the runbook.

Attachments (stubs): kvcache-warmup-log-20261002.txt; redwood-vpc-dryrun-checklist-v1.pdf (updated)

Drive folder: https://drive.redwood.company/drive/folders/ops/vpc-dryrun-2026-10-02
Jira: INF-4821 (linked: add probe + alert)

Thanks team — clean run. Will schedule a short retro if EdgeCloud wants to validate the multi-peering case.

Mina
Ops Engineer, Redwood Inference

Summary from the 13:00 PT dry run (short readout):
- Network: EdgeCloud performed BGP reset at 13:03 — observed transient route convergence ~9s. No packet loss >1% post-convergence.
- KV-cache warmup: followed Irene's recommendation: 10 minutes single-stream priming then a 60s 40-concurrency spike. Result: steady-state hit ratio reached 78% after ~6 minutes of priming; spike caused temporary batch queue increase but no 5xx. Logs attached.
- Smoke tests: auth, simple-infer, telemetry ingestion all passed. One telemetry probe lag (~11s) during route convergence but recovered.
- Fallback exercise: manual fallback to hosted-model variant was exercised at 13:35 by Sean — traffic shift confirmed and validation traces show successful failover within 18s.

title: Discovery follow-up: integration scope, SDK preferences, and PoC milestones
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Price <jordan@eunoiahealth.com>, Priya Menon <priya.menon@eunoiahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery follow-up: integration scope, SDK preferences, and PoC milestones

Hi Jordan & Priya —

Thanks for taking the time today. Quick recap from our side and suggested next steps so we keep momentum.

Top pain points you called out:
- Latency variability on current in-house LLM infra impacting clinician workflows (95th p99 ~ 1.2s bursty).
- High per-token inference cost when traffic spikes (billing unpredictability for pilot budgets).
- Need for predictable data controls (PHI flow & auditability).

What success looks like (agreed):
- Sub-200ms median token latency in the clinician UI for common prompts.
- <30% reduction in per-inference cost for high-throughput batch jobs vs current baseline.
- Vetted private deployment option (VPC) with KMS key control and audit logs retained 1 year.

Current stack you shared:
- Frontend: React (Next.js) calling backend via GraphQL.
- Backend: Java (Spring Boot) microservices on GCP (us-west1).
- Existing ML infra: an internal PyTorch-based service + off-the-shelf embeddings.

Integration preferences we noted:
- SDKs: primary preference for JavaScript/TypeScript (server-side) + a Java client for backend calls.
- API needs: streaming chat responses + function-calling-style structured outputs for downstream routing.
- Authentication: prefer service account key with short-lived tokens, and ability to integrate with GCP KMS for customer-managed keys.

Proposed PoC scope & timeline (slice to validate core pain points):
1) Week 0 (this week) — sign MoA & technical contacts, exchange infra/whitelist details. Owner: Jordan / Connor.
2) Week 1 — sandbox account provisioned, Redwood hosts shared test model, sample workload run. Deliverable: perf baseline vs your baseline. Owner: Connor.
3) Week 2–3 — integrate SDK snippets into a staging backend route (JS + Java), enable streaming, run 1k synthetic clinical prompts. Deliverable: latency and cost report. Owner: Priya / Marissa.
4) Week 4 — private deployment feasibility review (networking/KMS). Deliverable: integration checklist and draft runbook. Owner: Sean.

Next concrete asks from Eunoia (if you can confirm) by EOD Tue 9/21:
- Primary technical contact for Priya to pair with during integration (name + email).
- Sample prompt set (CSV) for production-like latency testing.

Attachments: integration checklist (light), PoC timeline PDF.

If that all matches your takeaways I can send a calendar invite for a short kickoff (30–45m) next Thursday (9/23) 10:00–10:45 PT.

Thanks again — happy to tighten any items.

Best,
Connor O'Brien
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0147

Attachment(s): redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx, poC-timeline-v1.pdf


From: Jordan Price <jordan@eunoiahealth.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Menon <priya.menon@eunoiahealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Sep 2026 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery follow-up: integration scope, SDK preferences, and PoC milestones

Connor —

Appreciate the clear notes. Summary aligns with what we heard internally. A couple clarifications:
- The 200ms target is aggressive but a good stretch goal for median; for the PoC let's set both median and p95 targets (median <200ms, p95 <500ms).
- For cost, our benchmark is current average $0.012 per inference; we aim for <= $0.009 for production.

Actionables from our side:
- Primary technical contact: Priya Menon (priya.menon@eunoiahealth.com), Sr. Platform Eng. She'll own SDK integration.
- I'll upload a 250-line prompt CSV to the Drive folder you shared by Monday EOD.
- We are ok with Redwood providing a hosted sandbox for performance testing; would like options for Dedicated later if PoC looks good.

Kickoff: 9/23 10:00 PT works for us. Please send invite.

Thanks,
Jordan
VP Product | Eunoia Health

preview:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Li <jordan.li@acme.ai>, Eliot Markus <eliot@brightworks.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Request: compress walkthrough to match CTO evening window (backup coverage included)

Jordan / Eliot —

Quick note: Jordan flagged that Acme's CTO (Victor Shen) will be available only briefly on Wednesday evening (local PT) due to travel — he can join for ~30–40 minutes between 17:30–18:15 PT. Given that, can we compress the demo so Victor can see the core architecture + TRL and we follow up with a short deep-dive for engineering next week?

Two options I want to propose (both attached in the updated agenda):

title: Service-account discovery + SCIM sampling plan for Medicore SOC2 intake
preview:
From: Amelia Rogers <amelia.rogers@medicore.com>
To: connor.obrien@redwood.com
Cc: rachel.kim@redwood.com, tessa.morgan@redwood.com
Date: Wed, 9 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intake: scoped ask for service accounts + SCIM sample for SOC2

Hi Connor and team,

Thanks again — Medicore's SOC2 intake is moving faster than expected and the auditors asked for two focused artifacts to help them validate provisioning controls: a sample of SCIM delta events covering a 7-day window, and a current inventory of service accounts (non-human principals) including owner/owner-team and last-activity timestamp.

Can Redwood produce a small SCIM delta CSV (7 days) and a scoped service-account export? We only need a sample of ~250 deltas and the most active service accounts (top 50 by API calls) for initial review. They also requested a short runbook describing the methodology you used to identify service accounts (what queries, heuristics, logs).

Deliverables requested by auditors:
- SCIM delta sample CSV (7-day window) with timestamps, action type (create/update/delete), user id, externalId, attribute changes
- Service-account inventory CSV with service account id, name, owner, owner-team, created_at, last_seen
- Short runbook (PDF) describing discovery heuristics and any caveats

Target: Can we get these together by end-of-day Friday PT? Auditors will accept redacted identifiers if needed, but they want full metadata for ownership.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — happy to jump on a short call if helpful.

Thanks,
Amelia Rogers
Security Ops, Medicore Health

-----Original Message-----

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Rogers <amelia.rogers@medicore.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 10:05:23 -0700
Subject: Re: Intake: scoped ask for service accounts + SCIM sample for SOC2

Hi Amelia — thanks for the clear list. We can produce this. Quick notes on scope and approach:

1) SCIM deltas: we'll export a 7-day delta stream from our SCIM ingestion logs starting 2026-09-02 00:00:00 PT through 2026-09-08 23:59:59 PT. We will sample the delta file to ~250 entries prioritizing unique externalIds and varied action types. We'll include an audit column that points to the ingestion job id.

2) Service-account discovery: we run a heuristic that combines options: principal_type flag in the provisioning record, token-only credentials (no human owner pairing), and highest request-rate principals over the last 90 days. We'll produce the top 50 by API calls and include last_seen.

3) Redaction: we can mask email addresses but auditors request owner metadata — we suggest preserving owner name and team but hash the service-account id and externalId. Confirm if that is acceptable.

4) Runbook: short PDF (1-2 pages) describing queries, log sources (SCIM ingestion, authentication logs), and known gaps (third-party syncs that bypass SCIM).

Action items for us:
- Produce the SCIM delta sample CSV (connor)
- Export service-account-inventory.csv (rachel)
- Draft runbook + methodology (tessa)

I'll assemble the artifacts and drop them into a shared Drive folder and share the link. If auditors need full identifiers for a small subset, we can provide after a documented NDA with Medicore.

Connor O'Brien
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

-----Original Message-----

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Amelia Rogers <amelia.rogers@medicore.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 08:22:41 -0700
Subject: Re: Intake: scoped ask for service accounts + SCIM sample for SOC2

All, quick status update:
- Attached a scoped export draft: service-account-inventory.csv (sample rows only). Column list: service_account_id, display_name, owner_name, owner_team, created_at, last_seen, token_type.
- I ran the discovery query against the provisioning DB and auth logs. There are ~1,100 candidate service principals; filtering to top 50 by API calls is straightforward.
- One caveat: ~8% of principals are created via an external SSO sync that does not emit externalId consistently; we'll note that in the runbook.

Amelia — is owner_name + owner_team sufficient (instead of full email)? We can include owner email if auditors insist.

Attaching: [Attachment: service-account-inventory.csv (csv)]

Rachel Kim
Director, Applied Security Controls, Redwood

-----Original Message-----

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Rogers <amelia.rogers@medicore.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, SOC2 Audit Team <soc2-audit@3p-audits.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 15:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intake: scoped ask for service accounts + SCIM sample for SOC2

Amelia — sending the artifacts now. Please note the Drive link below contains: the 7-day SCIM delta sample (scim_delta_sample.csv), the service-account inventory (service-account-inventory.csv), and the runbook (scim_recon_playbook_v1.pdf). We redacted service account IDs and hashed externalIds per our standard intake; owner name and team are preserved.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cReconPlaybook
HubSpot ticket: HS-52871
DocuSign (if needed for expanded identifiers): https://docusign.com/envelope/0a1b2c3d4e

If auditors need the raw, unredacted IDs for a small sample, please put the request in writing and we'll follow Medicore's consent process.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Head of Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: scim_delta_sample.csv (csv)]
[Attachment: scim_recon_playbook_v1.pdf (pdf)]

On forwarding: included the audit team on the last delivery for visibility.

title: CrescentCare: feasibility for combined HIPAA + GLBA addenda and data residency options
preview:
From: Dana Morales <dana.morales@crescentcare.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: CrescentCare Legal <legal@crescentcare.com>
Date: 2026-09-07T15:22:00-07:00
Subject: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the quick intro earlier — following up from our side. CrescentCare is evaluating Redwood for a pilot that will span both payment/financial workflows and PHI for certain clinics. Before we move to a demo/pilot we need to confirm a few items so our procurement and legal teams can greenlight the engagement: 

- Can Redwood sign both a HIPAA BAA and a GLBA addendum simultaneously (single-execution or parallel addenda)?
- What is the current subprocessors list and will any subprocessor process PHI?
- Data residency options: can we restrict storage/replication to US-only regions and/or get a Dedicated/Private deployment?
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II, encryption at rest/in transit, audit log retention windows (we typically need 1 year), and whether you support customer-managed KMS in Private deployments.

I've attached a short questionnaire (CrescentCare_DPA_Questions.xlsx) with some specifics from procurement. Our legal lead Evan (cc'd) will be the primary reviewer.

Would appreciate a brief call this week to go over feasibility and a sketch of the proposed commercial path (pilot + addenda). 

Thanks,
Dana Morales
Head of Vendor Risk
CrescentCare Financial

Attachments: CrescentCare_DPA_Questions.xlsx (spreadsheet, 14KB)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Dana Morales <dana.morales@crescentcare.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-07T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Hi Dana — great to meet you over email. Thanks for sending the questionnaire. I've looped in Laura (legal) and Monica (security) so we can answer the details and propose a path forward. Quick summary up front: 

- Redwood supports execution of multiple addenda; we can either add GLBA-specific language as an addendum to our DPA or execute discrete addenda in parallel — we typically aim to consolidate where possible to keep negotiations simpler.
- Subprocessor list: Monica will attach our current subprocessors and flag any that touch indexing/analytics rather than PHI.
- Data residency: we offer Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) and a Private deployment (customer VPC/on-prem) that meet US-only residency requirements; hosted accounts can be constrained to US regions as well but we recommend Dedicated/Private for stronger contractual guarantees.
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II (we can share the report under NDA), encryption in transit & at rest, and configurable audit log retention in Dedicated/Private. Customer-managed KMS is available in Private deployments.

I'm proposing a 30-min technical + legal sync tomorrow or Thursday. Proposed slots: 09:00-10:00 PT or 13:30-14:30 PT. If those don't work, suggest a few times and we'll accommodate.

I've attached a starter addendum draft for your team to review (DPA_Addendum_PROPOSED.docx) — it's intentionally conservative to speed review.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: DPA_Addendum_PROPOSED.docx (word, 42KB)
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/CR-2026-342

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-07T17:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Looping internal stakeholders so we can decide negotiating posture and redlines. Key asks from CrescentCare that will need legal detail or product confirmation: 

- Combined execution: acceptable to do a single DPA with GLBA-specific scheds, but CrescentCare may insist on GLBA-prescriptive language around financial data handling and additional audit rights.
- Audit logs / retention: they want 1 year by default — we need to confirm default retention windows and whether we can extend retention for a customer at a cost.
- Subprocessors & PHI: important to be explicit which subprocessors (if any) will ever see PHI. If using Dedicated/Private and on-prem credentials, we can limit subprocessor scope.
- KMS: confirm customer-managed KMS in Private and whether key escrow is required for any operational needs.
- SLA / liability: they may request higher liability caps for financial/health data.

Monica: can you attach the current subprocessor list and the dataflow diagram for this engagement? I want to see whether analytics/metrics processes will touch tokens or raw inputs.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference

--- Forwarded attachment: DataFlowDiagram_v2.pdf (diagram, 88KB)

On 2026-09-07 16:45, Karthik Iyer wrote:
> Hi Dana — great to meet you over email...


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-08T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Laura / team — attaching the current subprocessors export and a short summary inline. Highlights: 

- Subprocessors that handle any persisted request content: 
  * VectorStore Inc. (indexing/search) — processes tokenized inputs for embeddings; currently we can configure whether embeddings storage is enabled for a customer.
  * MetricsCloud (telemetry) — ingests anonymized telemetry only; we can exclude telemetry on Dedicated/Private.
  * CloudStorageCo (S3-compatible) — object storage for model artifacts/backups; does not receive raw PHI in typical flows but backups may include snapshots unless in Private.

- For PHI/financial data: our recommended path is Dedicated or Private with customer-managed KMS. In that configuration we can ensure backups and KV caches are scoped to US regions and prevent cross-region replication.

- Audit logs: default retention in hosted is 90 days. Dedicated customers can extend to 365 days; Private deployments can meet whatever retention you operate. Retention extensions are configurable via an add-on.

- Tech constraints: some of the fastest kernels create ephemeral caches that we don't persist to external subprocessors — those stay within the serving runtime. We'll document which subsystems may touch token-level data.

Attaching: DataFlowDiagram_v2.pdf and current_subprocessors_20260908.csv

Monica Patel
Head of Security & Privacy Engineering
Redwood Inference

Attachments: DataFlowDiagram_v2.pdf (diagram, 88KB); current_subprocessors_20260908.csv (csv, 6KB)

From: Dana Morales <dana.morales@crescentcare.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <e.cho@crescentcare.com>, CrescentCare Legal <legal@crescentcare.com>
Date: 2026-09-09T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Thanks — helpful summary. A few clarifications before our legal team schedules a contract review: 

1) For VectorStore Inc: will embeddings storage be opt-in per customer and can it be disabled for our account? We cannot have external storage of PHI embeddings.
2) Retention: we need confirmation in writing that Dedicated + Private options support 365d audit logs and no cross-region replication for customer data.
3) KMS: for Private, is customer-managed KMS supported without key escrow? We prefer keys not be held by Redwood.

Also attaching a signed copy of the NDA and a completed version of the questionnaire — hope that speeds SOC 2 exchange. One of our legal folks will send a DocuSign routing for a limited addendum if initial answers match.

On Mon, Sep 7, 2026 at 4:45 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Dana — great to meet you over email. Thanks for sending the questionnaire...

Best,
Dana Morales
Head of Vendor Risk
CrescentCare Financial

Attachments: CrescentCare_DPA_Questions_COMPLETED.xlsx (spreadsheet, 20KB); CrescentCare_NDA_signed.pdf (pdf, 24KB); DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/link/abc123

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Dana Morales <dana.morales@crescentcare.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-10T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Vendor addenda + residency questions (HIPAA + GLBA)

Dana — thanks, and appreciate the completed questionnaire and NDA. Quick answers and next steps: 

- Embeddings: yes — embeddings storage is opt-in per customer. For your pilot we will disable external embeddings storage by default and can run in-memory-only or Dedicated-backed storage scoped to your tenant. We'll document the exact subsystems that persist embeddings.
- Retention & residency: we can confirm in writing that Dedicated and Private deployments support 365d audit log retention and can be configured to avoid cross-region replication for customer data. We'll include that language in the addendum.
- Customer-managed KMS: supported in Private deployments with no Redwood-held escrow by default. We'll provide the KMS integration guide for your security team.

Proposed next steps: 
- Legal: Laura will take the attachment and propose redlines to the DPA_Addendum_PROPOSED.docx and return a clean tracked draft within 3 business days.
- Security: Monica will share the redacted SOC 2 under NDA and the subprocessor CSV.
- Commercial: I'll schedule a 30-min call (technical + legal) for Thursday 09:00 PT. Please confirm availability or send preferred times.

I'll also open the HubSpot deal to track artifacts (CR-2026-342) and will send the DocuSign link once Laura signs off on the initial redlines.

Thanks everyone — looking forward to getting this scoped and into a pilot.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Laura / team — attaching the current subprocessors export and a short summary inline. Highlights: 

- Subprocessors that handle any persisted request content: 
  * VectorStore Inc. (indexing/search) — processes tokenized inputs for embeddings; currently we can configure whether embeddings storage is enabled for a customer.
  * MetricsCloud (telemetry) — ingests anonymized telemetry only; we can exclude telemetry on Dedicated/Private.
  * CloudStorageCo (S3-compatible) — object storage for model artifacts/backups; does not receive raw PHI in typical flows but backups may include snapshots unless in Private.

- For PHI/financial data: our recommended path is Dedicated or Private with customer-managed KMS. In that configuration we can ensure backups and KV caches are scoped to US regions and prevent cross-region replication.

- Audit logs: default retention in hosted is 90 days. Dedicated customers can extend to 365 days; Private deployments can meet whatever retention you operate. Retention extensions are configurable via an add-on.

- Tech constraints: some of the fastest kernels create ephemeral caches that we don't persist to external subprocessors — those stay within the serving runtime. We'll document which subsystems may touch token-level data.

Attaching: DataFlowDiagram_v2.pdf and current_subprocessors_20260908.csv

Redwood Inference

title: Streaming chat degradation during evening peak - customer impact
preview:
From: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: platform@careline.com
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 18:12:00 -0700
Subject: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Hi Kimberly,

We saw significant user-facing issues tonight between ~18:00-19:30 PDT on our CareLine chat. Several sessions reported partial streaming responses (client receives first few events, then stream stalls indefinitely). P95/P99 latency for SSE connections spiked ~4-8x and we observed repeated 502/504s from the Redwood streaming endpoint.

Attached are two quick screenshots and a sample service trace (trace_sample_stream_1.txt). This is impacting our customer support flow and one large enterprise customer is escalating. Can you confirm if this is a Redwood-side regression or something in our usage pattern?

Please advise ASAP — we may need to pause a campaign if this continues tomorrow.

Thanks,
Adrian Soto
Platform Engineer, CareLine
[Attachment: metrics_surge_20260907.png (png)]
[Attachment: trace_sample_stream_1.txt (txt)]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 18:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Adrian — thanks for the quick heads up and the artifacts. Sorry you're seeing this.

Short answers up front: we are seeing an unusual SSE tail-latency pattern starting last evening that lines up with your timeframe. I've looped in Rishi (serving/runtime) and Marissa (CS) to triage and coordinate with you.

Immediate asks from our side:
- Can you confirm traffic volume (connections/sec) and average tokens per response for the impacted sessions?
- If you have a client-side capture of the stream frames (small HAR or tcpdump), that helps.

We'll run a quick mitigation (temporary routing to a reserve capacity pool and non-stream fallback) while we investigate root cause. I'll update within 30 minutes.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: careline_screenshot_timeout_0915.png (png)]

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 18:37:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Thanks for the ping. I pulled traces from the serving runtime and I see recurring stalls during chunk flush for SSE sockets originating from our hosted region-us-west-2 pool between 18:05-19:10 PDT. p95 latency for stream-chunk flush is up and some connections see repeated keepalive renewals without progress.

Preliminary hypotheses:
- Prefix cache eviction pressure causing KV cache reload stalls under concurrent long-history chat sessions.
- A kernel-optimizer path triggered by a specific sequence length distribution under peak batching that yields a blocking I/O on certain NVME hosts.

Short-term mitigations I'm pushing now:
1) Enable temporary autoscale override to add 4x burst nodes to region-us-west-2 (10m).
2) Route CareLine's streaming token routing to the reserve pool to avoid the congested bin.
3) Enable non-streaming fallback for sessions that fail two stream chunks in a row (return last stable partial + poll-based continuation).

I'll follow up with targeted traces and a diff of the runtime metrics in 45 minutes.

—Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 18:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Adrian — checking in from CS. Customer impact summary for our records: 
- Window: 2026-09-08 18:05-19:30 PDT
- Symptom: SSE/WebSocket stream stalls -> partial messages, occasionally 502/504
- User-visible: latency spike, some UI clients hang and retry

We will open a support incident (HS-98765) and log the request for potential credits if the SLA is not met. Can you share whether your side retried connections aggressively or if clients maintained single long-lived connections? That helps determine whether prefix cache churn is the driver.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 19:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Rishi — ops runbook updates: I've executed the autoscale override and confirmed 3 reserve nodes are in-play (adding ~2x streaming throughput headroom). I also pushed a temporary routing rule for the CareLine tenant to the reserve tenant pool.

Telemetry notes: we see a correlated increase in NVME I/O wait and batched flush latencies on a small subset of hosts (host group sg-rt-12). I'm standing up targeted profiler jobs on those hosts; if I see repeated kernel stalls I'll evict the hosts from the pool for a hot fix.

Customer mitigation options we discussed with Kimberly/Marissa:
- Short-term: route to reserve pool + non-streaming fallback (deployed)
- Mid-term: enable configurable session-level max-history trimming for CareLine to reduce KV cache pressure

I'll keep monitoring; will escalate to infra lead if eviction required.

—Sean
Sean Gallagher
SRE, Redwood Inference

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 9 Sep 2026 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Executive visibility — CareLine streaming disruption and next steps

Team — looping in for executive visibility. Thanks for the quick triage. Please summarize the impact (users affected, estimated downtime window) and include an ask for whether we should approve a one-off credit for CareLine (they're a mid-enterprise account in renewal later this quarter).

Specific asks for update within 2 hours: 
1) Root-cause hypothesis and next steps for a durable fix (Rishi)
2) Estimated customer impact metrics (Marissa)
3) Ops mitigation status and timeline to full remediation (Sean)
4) Recommended customer-facing language and credit/compensation posture (Kimberly)

We should also consider a post-incident regression test focused on streaming chunk flush under heavy median-history workloads.

—Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
VP, Reliability, Redwood Inference

From: Adrian Soto <adrian.soto@careline.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: platform@careline.com
Date: Wed, 9 Sep 2026 10:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Streaming responses stalling during 6-8pm PST peak

Thanks for the rapid response and for pushing the reserve nodes — we saw immediate improvement after the route change. Affected customers were around 120 concurrent agents and several hundred end-users (session concurrency ~2.1k).

We accepted the non-streaming fallback in the short term. We'll try trimming session history on our side tomorrow as suggested. Please keep me posted on the durable fix timeline and any credits/compensation decisions.

Adrian
Adrian Soto
Platform Engineer, CareLine

title: Follow-up: data handling confirmation & opt-out for model improvements
preview:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@medicaai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-07T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up: data handling confirmation & opt-out for model improvements

Hi Priya — thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap from our call and proposed next steps so we have everything on record: 

- Your ask: MedicaAI will opt out of any training or model-improvement workflows that use customer data processed through Redwood.
- Logging/retention: you need access to request logs for troubleshooting; you indicated a preference for a 90-day retention window for request-level metadata (no PII in logs).
- Residency: production inference will remain in us-west1; logs may be stored in the same region unless otherwise requested.

What we can commit to today (subject to the addendum):
1) No data will be used to train or tune models on any hosted or shared Redwood model variants for MedicaAI.
2) We can configure request logging to redact user-provided PII fields and retain non-PII metadata for up to 90 days.
3) We will provide an audit checklist and evidence (SOC2 artifacts and sanitized log samples) for your compliance team.

Next steps I propose:
- I’ll share a brief addendum draft for legal to review (DocuSign-ready).
- We’ll attach an audit-evidence checklist and examples of the sanitized logs for your security team to validate.
- Schedule a short technical call (30 min) to walk your infra team through the log redaction and retention controls.

Attachments: privacy-ack-optout.pdf (draft addendum), audit-evidence-checklist.xlsx

If that matches what you heard, please reply with confirmation and any changes to the retention window or residency requirement.

Thanks — talk soon.

Dylan Brooks
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
D +1 415-555-0123

Can you also indicate whether the addendum can be executed via DocuSign and how long your legal team typically needs to sign? We hope to have a countersigned addendum within two weeks.
