title: Clarify consumption vs first-license surcharge for private VPC deployment
preview:
From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@mediq.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Lina Morales <procurement@mediq.io>
Date: Tue, 01 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing follow-up — VPC license & platform fees

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement at Mediq asked for a short write-up about how your pricing works for an on-prem (VPC-hosted) installation — specifically two items: 1) how the first-license "consumption buffer" surcharge works versus ongoing metered consumption, and 2) whether the platform fee is a one-time onboarding surcharge or a recurring monthly charge. They also asked whether we can cap the platform surcharge for the first year.

Could you send a two-line summary that I can drop into our procurement packet? Also attaching the draft SOW we have — we'd like to finalize commercial terms before their Q4 budget approval.

Best,
Jordan
CISO, Mediq Health

Attachment: Mediq_SOW_v2.pdf (application/pdf, 1.1MB)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@mediq.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing follow-up — VPC license & platform fees

Jordan — quick response below and I can follow up with more detail. Short summary for procurement: 

- First-license consumption buffer is a one-time provisioning surcharge (covers reserved capacity, image packaging, and a 30-day burst buffer). It is invoiced on signing and capped at the agreed amount in the SOW.
- Platform fee is recurring (monthly) and covers control-plane, observability retention, and patch orchestration; we can provide a discounted ramp in months 1–12 for customers committing to a 12+ month support tier.

If you want a single-line option to paste: "Initial provisioning surcharge (one-time) + recurring platform fee (monthly), with a first-year ramp discount available under a 12-month commitment."

Two quick clarifications to help negotiations: the "consumption buffer" is not usage billing — after the first 30 days, consumption is metered separately (per-token / per-embedding or per-node-hour depending on chosen licensing). Also, platform fee discounts are typically structured as % off for months 1–12 tied to a minimum committed node count.

I can join a call with procurement Thursday if helpful.

— Ava
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

Attachment: license_matrix_ava_review.xlsx (spreadsheet, 120KB)

From: Lina Morales <procurement@mediq.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@mediq.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ethan Rhodes <legal@mediq.io>
Date: Wed, 02 Sep 2026 08:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing follow-up — VPC license & platform fees

Thanks Ava — that helps. Procurement asks two follow-ups: (A) can Mediq get a firm cap on total first-year platform spend (provisioning surcharge + 12 months of recurring platform fee) so they can include it in their 2027 capital ask, and (B) how do support tiers map to response times and severity definitions (we need a table to include in the procurement packet). Legal will want any cap reflected as an appendix in the SOW.

Can you provide a capped number range (ballpark okay) and the support-tier bullets by EOD Friday?

Thanks,
Lina
Senior Procurement Manager, Mediq Health

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Lina Morales <procurement@mediq.io>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Sep 2026 13:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing follow-up — VPC license & platform fees

Lina — draft numbers below (ballpark ranges assuming 3-node private pool, Standard support and a modest token profile):

- One-time provisioning surcharge (consumption buffer): $45k — $60k (covers packaging, IP escrow prep, initial 30-day burst).
- Recurring platform fee (monthly): $6k — $9k per month.
- First-year capped spend (worst case): $117k — $168k (surcharge + 12 months platform fee).

Support tiers (summary):
- Standard: Business hours (M–F, 9–5) support, 8–24 hour response depending on Sev; patching windows scheduled monthly.
- Enhanced: 24/7 on-call for Sev1, 4-hour response for Sev1, 8-hour for Sev2, quarterly health checks and 8 hours of migration assistance.
- Enterprise: Dedicated TAM, guaranteed 2-hour Sev1 response, weekly ops reviews, unlimited runbook updates, and 40 hours of onboarding engineering.

Notes: We can convert first-license surcharge to a 12-month amortized credit if Mediq signs a 12+ month committed contract and pre-pays 6 months platform fee; we can include that as an appendix to the SOW.

Happy to convert these ranges into fixed-line items for the SOW appendix once you confirm node count and desired support tier.

— Marissa
Head of Commercials, Redwood Inference

From: Ethan Rhodes <legal@mediq.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Lina Morales <procurement@mediq.io>
Date: Mon, 06 Sep 2026 16:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing follow-up — VPC license & platform fees

Marissa / Ava — Legal reviewed and we like the amortization clause idea. Request two edits for the SOW appendix: (1) explicit definition of "provisioning surcharge" line items (engineering hours, packaging, escrow) with a not-to-exceed figure, and (2) a clear SLA table tying support tiers to response times and escalation contacts.

If you can send a redlined appendix by Wednesday we can fast-track our internal approval. Also confirm whether license transfer on hardware failure is included or priced separately.

Thanks — Ethan
Sr. Counsel, Mediq Health

---
Thread status: action items assigned to Redwood (deliver redlined appendix and license transfer note).

title: BrightBridge: VPC packaging, platform fee amortization & on-call retainer
preview:
From: Emily Tan <emily.tan@brightbridge.health>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@brightbridge.health, Matthew Lowe <matthew@cloudscale.io>
Date: 2026-08-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: VPC pricing ask — platform fee amortization and on-call options

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the demo last week. The executive team wants a clear commercial path for a private VPC deployment — specifically we need: a) how the platform fee can be amortized across the term, b) options for a migration/implementation retainer versus a time-and-materials approach, and c) on-call/SRE retainer tiers (what’s included vs. priced add-on).

Two constraints from procurement: BrightBridge prefers amortization over at least 24 months and a clear cap on year-over-year increases. We’re also evaluating whether a partial waiver of the migration retainer is available if we commit to a longer term or a minimum GPU-hour commitment.

Can you send an itemized example (platform fee, implementation retainer, support tiers) and a sample SOW? Also, procurement needs SOC2 evidence and a short list of compliance deliverables up front. I’ve attached a short compliance checklist.

Thanks — Emily
Emily Tan
Head of Cloud Strategy, BrightBridge Health
attachments: brightbridge-compliance-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Tan <emily.tan@brightbridge.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Matthew Lowe <matthew@cloudscale.io>
Date: 2026-08-30T11:01:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VPC pricing ask — platform fee amortization and on-call options

Hi Emily — great to hear from you and thanks for the checklist. I’ve attached a short commercial pack (BBH-MAP-v1.xlsx) and a draft SOW (SOW-draft-BBH.pdf) to give the team something tangible to review. High level options below — happy to walk through live.

1) Platform fee amortization (examples):
 - Standard amortization: platform fee paid upfront; we can amortize internally for you at 12, 24 or 36 month schedules for commercial purposes. Example numbers in the attached MAP show amortization over 24 months reduces the near-term cash hit by ~40% vs. upfront.
 - Cap on increases: we can cap platform fee increases at 5% year-over-year for the committed term.

2) Implementation / migration retainer:
 - Option A: Fixed migration retainer (recommended for predictable scope): covers project mgmt, runbook handoff, 8-week implementation window, knowledge transfer.
 - Option B: Reduced retainer + T&M: smaller upfront retainer with time-and-materials for scope creep; we can discount the T&M if you commit to 18+ months.

3) Support/on-call tiers (sample):
 - Standard (included): business-hours (M-F 09:00–17:00) email + ticket triage; 24‑hour P1 response via ticket.
 - Premium (add-on): 24/7 pager-backed on-call with SRE; guaranteed P1 RTO 4 hours; includes 2 emergency incident responses/year without extra charge.
 - Enterprise (SLA): Dedicated SRE block + runbook updates; priced as monthly retainer.

Suggested commercial compromise we’ve seen work: amortize the platform fee over 24 months, offer 50% off the migration retainer if customer commits to a 24-month term and a minimum committed GPU-hour band (see MAP). We can also include SOC2 artifacts and a short compliance packet — I’ve attached the SOC2 evidence request template (SOC2-evidence-request-2026.docx).

Next steps: I can turn the MAP into a single-page commercial summary for procurement. Would you prefer a 24-month amortization example or a 36-month one for your execs?

Best,
Avery Johnson
Head of Sales — Enterprise, Redwood Inference
attachments: BBH-MAP-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet); SOW-draft-BBH.pdf (doc)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-31T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: internal: margin / discount guidance for BBH

Avery / Sean — quick internal note before we finalize the summary for BrightBridge:
 - If we do 50% migration retainer discount + 24-month amortization, margin compresses ~6 points but still within acceptable band given potential expansion (Horizon workload).
 - Recommend gating the migration retainer waiver/discount on either: (a) 24-month term with 60% of expected commit in year 1, or (b) 18-month term + committed GPU-hour floor.
 - Consider offering a tiered SRE block (e.g., 40 hrs/month at discounted rate) for the first 6 months to reduce initial op-ex friction.

I’ll update the MAP to include a blended price column (post-discount) and a quick note for legal re: audit log retention (they asked about retention windows — default is 90 days; for longer retention we’ll need to add storage fee).

Marissa
Director, Commercial Strategy — Redwood Inference


From: Emily Tan <emily.tan@brightbridge.health>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@brightbridge.health, Matthew Lowe <matthew@cloudscale.io>
Date: 2026-09-01T10:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VPC pricing ask — platform fee amortization and on-call options

Thanks Avery — this is helpful. A few follow-ups from procurement and cloud ops:

 - BrightBridge preference is 24-month amortization — please send the exact monthly cash flows for that schedule so our FP&A team can model.
 - For the migration retainer: procurement would consider a 50% reduction if we sign 18–24 months and include a minimum committed baseline for the first 12 months. Can you confirm a firm number for the reduced retainer and the baseline GPU hours?
 - On on-call: we like the Premium option but need to know the exact scope of the 2 included emergency incidents (severity definition, time spent cap).
 - SOC2: procurement needs to see actual artifact examples; your SOC2 template is useful, but please share the last 12 months of third-party attestations or a portal link where we can pull redacted reports.

If you can get a clean commercial one-pager and the monthly amortization schedule by COB tomorrow we’ll route to legal and procurement for an initial read.

Emily


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Tan <emily.tan@brightbridge.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Matthew Lowe <matthew@cloudscale.io>
Date: 2026-09-01T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VPC pricing ask — platform fee amortization and on-call options

Emily — appreciate the specificity. Quick answers and a compromise proposal below; I’ll push a one-pager and the 24-month monthly cashflow table to your inbox by EOD.

 - 24-month amortization: will provide monthly schedule (platform fee / months). We can hold year-over-year increases to a maximum of 5% for the contracted term.
 - Reduced migration retainer: 50% discount available if BrightBridge signs a 24-month agreement and commits to a minimum of 120k GPU-hours in year 1 (numbers in the MAP attachment). Reduced retainer = $60k (vs. $120k full). If you prefer an 18-month term, we can do a $75k retainer with the same GPU-floor.
 - Premium on-call: covers 24/7 incident detection, pager escalation, and SRE-led incident remediation. The two included emergency incidents are defined as Sev 1 (service-impacting) and include up to 8 hours of SRE time each; additional hours billed at discounted T&M rate.
 - SOC2 artifacts: we can provide redacted attestations via a secure portal link; I’ve asked legal to prepare a redacted pack and will share the URL in the commercial one-pager.

Compromise suggested in the one-pager: 24-month amortization + $60k reduced migration retainer + 2 included Sev1 incident responses and a 10% committed-term discount on the recurring platform fee. If that aligns we’ll prepare an SOW and a term sheet for legal redlines.

Attaching updated SOW-draft-BBH.pdf (v2) for bracketed scopes and milestone payment schedule.

Thanks — Avery
attachments: SOW-draft-BBH.pdf (v2)


From: procurement@brightbridge.health
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Tan <emily.tan@brightbridge.health>, Matthew Lowe <matthew@cloudscale.io>
Date: 2026-09-02T16:05:00-07:00

title: Reserved capacity repricing and SKU sensitivity — Cerebellum Labs
preview:
From: Alicia Romero <alicia.romero@cerebellum.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-23T09:12:00Z
Subject: Reserved capacity and sensitivity to GPU pricing

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the call today. As we discussed, Cerebellum is planning a Q1 product launch that will materially increase inference volume. We are interested in a 12‑18 month reserved commitment but need better visibility on how discounts and final pricing move if GPU market prices shift.

Attached is a compressed usage feed and a quick forecast (cerebellum_usage_feed_aug2026.xlsx). Our ask: can Redwood produce a set of committed‑capacity pricing scenarios that show effective $/token given +/- 20% and +/- 40% changes in underlying GPU unit cost, and the approval path for any nonstandard discounts? Specifically: 
- A baseline 12‑month commitment at 5M tokens/day equivalent (break out by SKU mix if that helps)
- A 24‑month aggressive commit option at 8M tokens/day equivalent
- A two‑tier discount ladder tied to activation tranches (25% / 75% activation)

We need this by EOD Tuesday so we can present options to procurement on Wednesday. Also flag any finance/legal escalations that would be required to accept a price protection clause.

Appreciate you and the team jumping on this.

—Alicia
Alicia Romero
VP Engineering, Cerebellum Labs
(Attachment: cerebellum_usage_feed_aug2026.xlsx)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Romero <alicia.romero@cerebellum.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-23T14:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity and sensitivity to GPU pricing

Alicia — thanks, got the file and the numbers look reasonable. Marcus and I will run a scenario set and outline the approval path. Quick notes up front:

- We can produce sensitivity tables showing effective $/token under variable GPU cost assumptions and two SKU mixes (standard quantized and high‑perf).
- For a 12 vs 24 month tenor, the material lever is our discount ladder plus a conditional GPU passthrough clause (indexation). We will present both a capped pass‑through and a fixed buffer approach (e.g., 10% floor / 20% cap) so procurement can choose risk posture.
- Nonstandard discounts (net >15% off list for a multi‑year commit) will need Sales Director + Finance pre‑approval; anything above 25% requires Exec approval (Sean) and legal signoff on contract safeguards.

Marcus — can you own the model and produce: 1) a spreadsheet with the sensitivity tables, 2) a short 1‑page executive summary PDF with recommended path and a default offer we can present (include activation tranche language), and 3) a list of approvals needed for each discount band? Alicia asked EOD Tuesday — that’s tight but doable if we prioritize.

Alicia — we’ll send a proposed deck and summary first thing Monday, and the detailed workbook by Tuesday EOD. If useful, Sean can join procurement call Wednesday for the ask.

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference
(Attachment referenced: proposed_commit_summary.pdf)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Romero <alicia.romero@cerebellum.ai>
Date: 2026-08-24T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity and sensitivity to GPU pricing — model and recommendation

Kim / Sean / Alicia — attaching the workbook (capacity_pricing_model_v2.xlsx) and summarized the key outputs below.

Executive summary (TL;DR):
- Baseline offer (12m, 5M tokens/day equivalent, mixed quantized SKU): list price = $0.012/token. Proposed committed effective price w/ 12m, 20% activation discount ladder = $0.0096/token (20% off list).
- Sensitivity to GPU unit cost: with a +20% GPU cost shock, our marginal cost pushes effective $/token to $0.0108 under current kernel efficiency assumptions (we absorb some variance through batching/caching but not all). At +40% shock, effective $/token = $0.0129 — i.e., list price parity.
- For 24m, 8M tokens/day: we can stretch to a 27% effective discount if we include an activation tranche (30% at signup, 50% at month 6) and commit to an indexation clause with a 12% floor / 25% cap on pass‑through. This requires Finance pre‑approval and an Exec discount signoff for the 25% band.

Recommended routing:
1) Offer A (safe): 12m, 5M/day, 20% standard discount, no indexation. Requires Sales Manager signoff.
2) Offer B (aggressive): 24m, 8M/day, 25% max discount with indexation buffer (12% floor / 25% cap). Requires Finance + Sean (Exec) approval + Legal to insert pass‑through language.
3) Offer C (pilot): 6m pilot with a one‑time rebate if activation >70% at month 3 — low risk, for quick launch without full multi‑year commit.

Approval matrix (short):
- 0–15% discount: Sales rep signoff only.
- 16–25% discount: Sales Director + Finance signoff.
- >25%: Executive approval (Sean) + Legal + Finance conditions.

Alicia — the workbook has the scenario tables by SKU (quantized / high‑perf) under +/-20% and +/-40% GPU cost movement. I flagged a few cells where pass‑through would be triggered under our proposed index. Please confirm which offer you want us to put into a formal commercial LOI.

(Attachment: capacity_pricing_model_v2.xlsx)

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Sales Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Romero <alicia.romero@cerebellum.ai>
Date: 2026-08-26T16:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Reserved capacity and sensitivity to GPU pricing — model and recommendation

Thanks for the fast turnaround, Marcus. I concur with the routing. Approve the following path:

- Move Offer B to commercial draft with the indexation clause (12% floor / 25% cap) and the activation tranches you outlined.
- Give provisional Exec signoff for up to 25% effective discount contingent on Finance inserting a cost‑protection escrow clause and Legal finalizing the index language.
- If Cerebellum asks for anything above 25%, route to me with expected P&L impact and proposed mitigation (e.g., time‑limited rebate, shorter minimum tenor, or stricter activation gating).

Kim — please coordinate with Legal (cc’ed via internal ticket) and Finance to get the draft LOI out to Alicia by COB Wednesday. Marcus — please include the scenario rows that show the company would still meet our minimum margin thresholds under +20% GPU cost.

Happy to join procurement if they push hard.

—Sean

- For 24m, 8M tokens/day: we can stretch to a 27% effective discount if we include an activation tranche (30% at signup, 50% at month 6) and commit to an indexation clause with a 12% floor / 25% cap on pass‑through. This requires Finance pre‑approval and an Exec discount signoff for the 25% band.

2) Offer B (aggressive): 24m, 8M/day, 25% max discount with indexation buffer (12% floor / 25% cap). Requires Finance + Sean (Exec) approval + Legal to insert pass‑through language.
3) Offer C (pilot): 6m pilot with a one‑time rebate if activation >70% at month 3 — low risk, for quick launch without full multi‑year commit.

- Legal lead: Marissa Cole will own MSA redlines and coordinate with your counsel.

Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 17:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Onboarding request: Ariba enrollment + PO ACK timing

Emily / Sean —

Thanks — I reviewed the Aurelia onboarding packet. I’ve drafted a narrow redline that captures the two carveouts as an Appendix A (data residency boundary and shortened audit lookback for production tokens). That keeps the main MSA language intact and reduces risk for both sides.

High-level notes:
- Appendix approach: adds carveouts without altering indemnities or the core SLA language.
- Audit window: proposing 90 days for production telemetry access (Aurelia wanted 12 months; we can agree to records retention export with a formal request process).
- PO-related clause: we need an explicit Order Form exhibit that states PO acknowledgement is operational (not contractual) to avoid contract-level penalties for late ACK. If Aurelia requires strict contract relief for missed ACKs, we’ll need an SLA amendment tied to Dedicated capacity subscriptions.

I attached the redline (msa_redline_v2.docx). Emily — if your legal counterparty wants to talk through the Appendix approach I’m available tomorrow 10–11am ET.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
Attachments: msa_redline_v2.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 45KB)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Emily Turner <emily.turner@aureliahealth.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 08:20:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Onboarding request: Ariba enrollment + PO ACK timing

Emily —

Following up with the technical checklist and a proposed mapping approach:

- ACK window: We can support ACK within 24 hours for normal volume vendors; for higher-volume flows ( >500 POs/month) we recommend 48 hours and a batching approach.
- Integration options: synchronous ACK (near real-time) or nightly batched ACK CSV back to Ariba. We’ll need to know whether Aurelia’s Ariba instance expects an immediate PO-ACK webhook or a file ingest.
- PO fields: our template requires PO number, line-level quantities, unit price, tax codes, ship-to location ID, and order type. If Aurelia includes a custom SKU namespace we’ll need a mapping table prior to go-live.

Operational ask: please confirm the expected monthly PO volume and whether Aurelia needs contract-level penalties for missed ACKs. If penalties are required we’ll route to Product for SLA review; otherwise we’ll handle within the activation runbook.

I’ve created a mutual action plan skeleton and uploaded it here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aBcExampleDriveLink (mutual_action_plan.xlsx). Sean — once legal confirms the appendix approach I’ll schedule a 60-minute mapping call with Aurelia’s integration team.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Customer Integrations | Redwood Inference


From: Emily Turner <emily.turner@aureliahealth.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding request: Ariba enrollment + PO ACK timing

Sean / Marissa / Kimberly —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Aurelia legal is OK to accept an Appendix A for the two carveouts provided the audit access process is explicit. Our ops team confirms the Ariba instance expects near-real-time ACKs (webhook preferred) but we can accept nightly batches during the pilot. Expected volume to start is ~120 POs/month (small pilot).

We don’t require contract-level penalties for missed ACKs during pilot — that would be in scope for a volume-based SLA later. Marissa — our counsel would like the 90-day audit lookback in the appendix but also an explicit escalation path for emergency access requests; can we add that?

Please prepare the DocuSign envelope for the Order Form once the Appendix language is finalized. Also happy to book Marissa for a quick call tomorrow 10am ET to walk through the redline.

Best,
Emily Turner
Senior Vendor Manager | Aurelia Health

title: Urgent: p99 spike and throughput drop — suspected stale prefix cache
preview:
From: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops-team@orbitpay.com
Date: 2026-08-22T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Ben —

We started seeing a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency and a handful of timeouts starting ~08:45 PT. Our service health page shows p99 jumping from ~220ms to >2s and a corresponding error-rate bump (5xxs) on the hosted endpoint serving our traffic. Throughput is also lower — we see requests backing up with 429 retries. This started right after your latest rollout this morning (we were on v2026-08-22-03).

Can you confirm whether anything changed on your side (cache settings, model variant routing, prefix invalidation)? This is impacting checkout flows. We need an ETA and mitigation plan.

— Jordan
Director of Product, OrbitPay

---
Attachment: slo-p95-graph.png (image/png)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Jordan — thanks for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We see the same symptoms in our dashboards. I'm escalating to our SRE team and product owner immediately.

Quick notes:
- We pushed a small infra config tweak at 08:55 (intended to tighten prefix TTLs for cache churn) but that was targeted at an internal infra shard; we believed it would be isolated.
- I'm CC'ing Marissa (SRE lead) and Sean (Customer Success) to coordinate.

I'll share a short status update in the next 20 minutes with what we find and a temporary mitigation (rollback / bypass) if needed.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Summary of initial triage (SRE):
- Observed p95/p99 regressions starting ~08:46; p99 peak ~2.3s for OrbitPay's key route (/v1/generate).
- Error rate (5xx) correlated with cache-miss amplification: a large set of very similar prefix keys experienced simultaneous eviction around 08:55. We see a burst of KV miss lookups followed by slower cold-path model loads.
- Timeline: config change (infra) rolled at 08:55 that reduced prefix-TTLs from 6h to 30m on the affected pool. There was an unintended broad scope flag — it applied to the hosted pool serving OrbitPay traffic.
- Evidence: attached prefix-eviction-log.txt (excerpt) shows mass evictions and subsequent model load traces with request IDs and latencies. Also linking dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/OrbitPay/p95-heatmaps

Immediate mitigation options we've started:
1) Rollback TTL change to previous value (6h) — being prepared now (impact: quick fix for cache behavior, low-risk).
2) Enable prefix warm-up (proactive KV prefetch for hot prefixes) — longer to fully ramp.
3) Route OrbitPay to a healthy pool (requires routing policy change + ephemeral capacity) — takes ~25 minutes.

We're executing step 1 now. I'll provide confirmation once rollback completes and we'll monitor p95/p99. If the rollback doesn't restore the p99 within 15 minutes we'll trigger 2 and 3.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: prefix-eviction-log.txt (text/plain)


From: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Thanks for the quick reply and for starting a rollback. Two asks from our side:
1) OrbitPay legal/ops need a short RCA and confirmation of whether any user data was retried or duplicated (we saw some duplicate tokens generated due to retries).
2) We will need a formal credit request / SLA escalation if the incident duration exceeds 45 minutes. Please loop in someone from leadership if you expect >30 minutes.

Also please confirm whether the rollout change had a changelog/PR and why the scope wasn't constrained to internal shards. We're happy to be on a joint post-mortem.

Jordan
---


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Exec / customer update: mitigation steps and ask for leadership sync

Team —

Thank you for the rapid triage. Status update for orbit pay execs and our leadership:
- Root cause (prelim): unintended broad TTL reduction on prefix cache pool during an infra config rollout at 08:55. This triggered simultaneous prefix eviction which magnified cold-start model loads and led to p99 regressions and transient 5xxs.
- Actions completed:
  * 10:10 — Rolled back TTL to 6h for affected pool (rollback completed, monitoring).
  * 10:25 — Enabled temporary prefix-warmup for top 5 hot prefixes for OrbitPay traffic.
  * 11:00 — Scoped routing rule created; traffic will be moved to alternate pool if we see another spike.
- Current state (16:40): p95 returned to ~280ms, p99 at ~380ms (still elevated vs baseline but much improved). Error-rate back down to baseline.

Customer impact: 40 minutes of elevated p99, several transient timeouts leading to retries. OrbitPay has indicated they'll request SLA credit if >45min. Please preserve all logs and the post-incident timeline for the RCA.

Requests / next steps:
- Sean/GTM: coordinate customer comms and credit process (Sean, please draft a short timeline and proposed remediation credits).
- Maya/Infra: implement a guard to prevent global TTL flag scope changes without a canary/owner approval; propose an emergency rollback runbook.
- Marissa: finish the deep dive and produce an RCA (include why eviction set was larger than expected and whether prefix key hashing caused hot-key collisions).
- Ben: schedule a call with Jordan and their ops lead tomorrow morning for a joint post-mortem.

I'll join the customer call if leadership presence is requested. We should treat this as a priority for the week — it's instructive for our rollout controls and cache safety nets.

Markus Klein
VP, Platform Engineering | Redwood Inference
Phone: +1-415-555-0199

- Evidence: attached prefix-eviction-log.txt (excerpt) shows mass evictions and subsequent model load traces with request IDs and latencies. Also linking dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/OrbitPay/p95-heatmaps

Immediate mitigation options we've started:
1) Rollback TTL change to previous value (6h) — being prepared now (impact: quick fix for cache behavior, low-risk).
2) Enable prefix warm-up (proactive KV prefetch for hot prefixes) — longer to fully ramp.
3) Route OrbitPay to a healthy pool (requires routing policy change + ephemeral capacity) — takes ~25 minutes.

We're executing step 1 now. I'll provide confirmation once rollback completes and we'll monitor p95/p99. If the rollback doesn't restore the p99 within 15 minutes we'll trigger 2 and 3.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
---

Date: 2026-08-22T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Thanks for the quick reply and for starting a rollback. Two asks from our side:
1) OrbitPay legal/ops need a short RCA and confirmation of whether any user data was retried or duplicated (we saw some duplicate tokens generated due to retries).
2) We will need a formal credit request / SLA escalation if the incident duration exceeds 45 minutes. Please loop in someone from leadership if you expect >30 minutes.

Also please confirm whether the rollout change had a changelog/PR and why the scope wasn't constrained to internal shards. We're happy to be on a joint post-mortem.

Jordan

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Exec / customer update: mitigation steps and ask for leadership sync

Team —

Thank you for the rapid triage. Status update for orbit pay execs and our leadership:
- Root cause (prelim): unintended broad TTL reduction on prefix cache pool during an infra config rollout at 08:55. This triggered simultaneous prefix eviction which magnified cold-start model loads and led to p99 regressions and transient 5xxs.
- Actions completed:
  * 10:10 — Rolled back TTL to 6h for affected pool (rollback completed, monitoring).
  * 10:25 — Enabled temporary prefix-warmup for top 5 hot prefixes for OrbitPay traffic.
  * 11:00 — Scoped routing rule created; traffic will be moved to alternate pool if we see another spike.
- Current state (16:40): p95 returned to ~280ms, p99 at ~380ms (still elevated vs baseline but much improved). Error-rate back down to baseline.

Customer impact: 40 minutes of elevated p99, several transient timeouts leading to retries. OrbitPay has indicated they'll request SLA credit if >45min. Please preserve all logs and the post-incident timeline for the RCA.

Requests / next steps:
- Sean/GTM: coordinate customer comms and credit process (Sean, please draft a short timeline and proposed remediation credits).
- Maya/Infra: implement a guard to prevent global TTL flag scope changes without a canary/owner approval; propose an emergency rollback runbook.
- Marissa: finish the deep dive and produce an RCA (include why eviction set was larger than expected and whether prefix key hashing caused hot-key collisions).
- Ben: schedule a call with Jordan and their ops lead tomorrow morning for a joint post-mortem.

I'll join the customer call if leadership presence is requested. We should treat this as a priority for the week — it's instructive for our rollout controls and cache safety nets.

Markus Klein
VP, Platform Engineering | Redwood Inference

title: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt
preview:
From: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt

Hey Ben,

Thanks again for the demo this morning — Samir and I were able to exercise the streaming SDK during a long-document summarization flow. We hit an issue when the combined prompt + context exceeded ~2.5k tokens: the client shows a streaming disconnect and the server returns a 502 briefly. Re-attaching minimal repro logs and a short video clip.

Questions: is there a known limit on streaming/session length? Is there a recommended client-side retry pattern we should use to avoid partial outputs? We're evaluating Redwood for a critical feature so any guidance helps.

Attachments: velvet_demo_logs.zip (200KB), screen-clip.mp4 (referenced)

—Maria
Head of Product, VelvetHost


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@velvethost.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt

Hi Maria — really appreciate the quick repro and video. I'm sorry you ran into that during the demo.

Short answer: we don't have a hard client-side token cap, but we do have operational safeguards at the routing/edge and within the runtime that can abort or retry when memory pressure or unexpected kernel failure occurs. That said, a 502 is not expected for a normal streaming session.

I'm looping in Logan (platform/runtime) and Marissa (sales eng) to get a quick triage and a recommended workaround while your team evaluates. Would you be available for a 30-min troubleshooting call tomorrow at 10:00am PT? If so, we'll attempt a live repro with your payload.

Action: attaching the logs you sent to our internal ticket and will follow up with the runtime team.

Best,
Ben
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T12:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — internal triage

Ben — thanks for the ping and logs. Quick read: the server-side traceback in velvet_demo_logs.zip points to an OOM-triggered bailout inside the attention kernel when the prefix + generation window hits a particular alignment (long-ish prefix + medium generation). We saw similar failure modes earlier this year but they were mitigated by a runtime patch that tightened buffer checks; looks like this example exercises a rarer alignment.

Immediate suggestions for the call:
- Ask Maria to reproduce with the SDK using the --no-prefetch flag to rule out client prefetch/KV cache growth as a trigger.
- Try trimming user prompt prefix to see if crash correlates with prefix length vs generation length.
- If we can reproduce, we'll run with debug kernels enabled and capture a detailed core/log snapshot.

I'll stand by for the 10am PT repro. If VelvetHost can share a sanitized request payload ahead of time, that will speed things up (we can sign an NDA for that sample if needed).

—Logan
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T12:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — next steps

Maria — thanks for agreeing to the call. Confirming 2026-08-20 10:00am PT for the repro session. For the workshop we'll:
- Attempt live repro with your saved payload and the SDK sample you used.
- If the SDK flags (prefetch/caching) matter, I'll try a quick client-side workaround to keep you unblocked for the POC.
- Logan will gather runtime traces if we repro.

Can you upload the sanitized request to the Drive link Ben shared or drop it into the HubSpot file area? If you prefer, we can use a short-term secrets-safe transfer (we can spin up an ephemeral S3 link).

TL;DR: we'll treat this as "field triage" rather than a guaranteed SLA incident; we'll aim to produce a repro and short-term mitigation within 48 hours.

—Marissa
Sales Engineering Lead


From: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@velvethost.com>
Date: 2026-08-20T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — demo repro call

Thanks team — on the call now and just uploaded the sanitized sample to the Drive link. The file is named repro_payload_sanitized.json. We're ready for a live run.

—Maria


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-21T16:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — post-triage notes

Quick sync notes from the repro today (internal):
- We reproduced the disconnect with the sanitized payload. Root cause: an off-by-one in buffer sizing for pooled attention buffers when prefix lengths align on a 512-token boundary with a mid-sized generation window. This leads to a transient write past end-of-buffer and the runtime triggers a controlled 502 abort.
- Short-term mitigation (deployed as a hotfix to the staging cluster during call): runtime now pads pooled buffers by +1 allocation for the affected kernels. This reduces throughput by ~0.6% but prevents the crash. Patch id: RT-HF-2026-0819.
- Recommended SDK guidance for customers until official release: set --no-prefetch or limit prefix to <2300 tokens when doing long prompt + generation mixes; enable client-side single-shot retry with idempotency token to resume safely. We'll document this in the KB article.
- Next steps: create an internal RFC to make padding permanent with a perf-cost analysis, and include a test case in the CI repro matrix for long-prefix alignments. Logan to own RFC; Marissa to draft customer-facing KB; Ben to update HubSpot deal notes.

Attachments included for the team: repro_steps.txt, screenshot-rt-disconnect.png

—Sean
Staff Sales Engineer (Operations Liaison)

title: Post-demo recap + criteria & validation plan
preview:
From: Kai Turner <kai.turner@greenhealthlabs.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Thanks for the demo today — quick clarifications

Jasmine,

Thanks for the walkthrough earlier — the demo helped our engineering and data teams see how Redwood routes and caches at the request layer. A few quick clarifications so we can align internally before the bakeoff: 

1) Data residency: we need confirmation that all inference logs for the PII-marked datasets can be kept in our VPC/private deployment and retained for 30 days.
2) Latency targets: for our triage workflow we need p95 < 250ms on short prompts (~120 tokens) and sustained 10 TPS for batched analyst requests.
3) Failure modes: Tom in infra asked what automatic fallback policy looks like when a pinned large model is unavailable. Do you have docs on the fallback chain?

We also want to capture the competitor notes you and your team mentioned — Marco from CloudServe and Priya from ModelStream will be participating in the bakeoff. Can you send a short test matrix for the POC and the artifacts we should run?

Thanks — I can coordinate internal test windows next week if you share the matrix.

Kai Turner
Head of Data Platform
GreenHealth Labs

Attachments: none


From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
To: Kai Turner <kai.turner@greenhealthlabs.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 17:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Thanks for the demo today — quick clarifications

Kai — great meeting, and appreciate the concrete asks. Summary and next steps below (short):

Pain points you called out:
- Regulatory/data residency: need VPC/on-prem retention for logs and audit trail (30d).
- Latency/SLA: target p95 < 250ms for 120-token bursts; 10 TPS sustained for batching flows.
- Reliability/fallback: want deterministic fallback sequence and observability for failover.

Goals for the bakeoff/POC:
- Validate p95 and throughput across short prompt and batched scenarios.
- Verify prefix/KV cache effectiveness for analyst workflow and overall unit cost delta.
- Confirm security controls: KMS integration, audit log exports, and SOC2 artifacts.

Competitive context (from our side):
- CloudServe (Marco): strong at request routing, less mature cache-aware batching.
- ModelStream (Priya): aggressive quantization options, unknown production fallbacks.

Proposed validation plan (attached):
- A: Latency microbench (120-token prompts) — p50/p95/p99 + token cost.
- B: Sustained throughput run (10–15 TPS) with real prompt mix for 30m.
- C: Failure injection: model unavailability + check fallback correctness and SLA reporting.
- D: Security checklist: KMS key rotation test + audit log export.

I've attached: 1) demo recording, 2) latency-test-script.sh (simple harness you can run in your test VMs). Sean and Marissa — please review the test matrix and confirm whether we need a dedicated capacity reservation for the runs (recommended) and if Legal should pre-seed an SOW ask for the POC.

Next steps (who/when):
- Jasmine: share Drive link with test artifacts + baseline scripts (today).
- Kai: propose two 2-hour test windows next week (Mon/Tue preferred).
- Sean: confirm dedicated pool availability and rough cost estimate.
- Marissa: flag any procurement/SOW gating items.

Jasmine Liu
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0119

Attachments: demo-recording-20260819.mp4, latency-test-script.sh

To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

Quick synthesis (why GH is a strong fit):
- Workload: mixed analyst + async triage; traffic pattern benefits from prefix caching + steady batching.
- Constraints: 30d retention in VPC, KMS integration, p95 latency target.
- Decision window: GH wants vendor results + test artifacts within 10 business days of POC.

Recommended Redwood approach:
1) Reserve a small Dedicated pool (2x A10G-equivalent) for the POC windows to avoid noisy neighbors. Sean — can you provision this as "GH-POC-A" and provide an estimated commit cost?
2) Execute the four-test matrix Jasmine shared (A–D). I'll own A and C; Sean will own B (throughput); Marissa will own D (security checklist and SOW gating).
3) Provide a simple results dashboard (p50/p95/cost per 1k tokens) and a short vendor-compare one-pager for GH procurement.

Open questions to resolve before tests:
- Marco (CloudServe) and Priya (ModelStream) — will they run the same harness or run their own agents? If they use their own, we need time-synced seed prompts and the same dataset snapshots.
- Legal: do we need an NDA amendment to run failure injection tests in their environment? Marissa — please advise.

Deliverables and timeline:
- By EOD today: Drive folder with scripts + test matrix (done; link below).
- By Mon Aug 24: GH proposes 2 test windows; we lock one and run tests by Aug 26.
- By Aug 28: Results + comparison one-pager delivered.

title: Solara Health: sustained 5xx/429 + retry storm — remediation & settlement discussions
preview:
From: Sara Li <ops@solarahealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <cto@solarahealth.com>, procurement@solarahealth.com
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: URGENT: Production 5xx/429 spike + retry storm impacting checkout

Aditya —

We observed a sustained elevation in 5xx and 429 responses across our checkout and patient-intake endpoints starting ~2026-08-16T23:45Z. The elevated errors and resultant client-side retry loops caused timeouts, doubled request volumes, and a backlog in our worker pool. This directly impacted 8% of checkout attempts in the window; revenue is at risk for today.

Attached is a short log excerpt (request_log_excerpt.csv) showing the error codes and timestamps. We need:
- immediate mitigation plan and ETA to restore normal error rates
- confirmation of root cause analysis timeline
- proposed customer remediation (credits/refund) and a formal SLA remediation offer

Raj (CTO) is on this thread; procurement is copied for billing follow-up. We need an exec-level commitment on credits if you confirm this is on Redwood. Please treat as P1.

Thanks,
Sara Li
Sr. Director, Site Reliability
Solara Health

--
Attachment: request_log_excerpt.csv (10 KB)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Sara Li <ops@solarahealth.com>

Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: URGENT: Production 5xx/429 spike + retry storm impacting checkout

Sara —

Really sorry you hit this. Thanks for the logs. I’ve escalated to our on-call ops and engineering leads and we’re standing up a war room now. Initial asks: can you share approximate volume in requests/minute during the spike and any client-side retry configuration you have? That’ll help with throttling proposals.

We’ll send a triage update in 30–45 minutes and a remediation timeline shortly after. I’ll also prep a commercial remediation proposal for procurement once we have root cause.

I’m on standby and will loop our head of sales (Marissa) and SRE lead (Hannah) to coordinate.

Aditya Rao
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
Direct: +1 (415) 555-0134



From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Li <ops@solarahealth.com>, Raj Patel <cto@solarahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: URGENT: Production 5xx/429 spike + retry storm impacting checkout

Aditya / team —

Quick triage update from SRE: we saw a cascading failure triggered by an autoscaler policy change deployed accidentally in the EU region that incorrectly classified long-tail requests as cold and scaled down inference workers while traffic spiked from Solara. Client-side retries (no jitter) amplified traffic and saturated the ingress queue, leading to 429s and downstream 5xx as requests timed out waiting for workers.

Mitigation applied (completed 09:30 PDT):
- Reverted autoscaler policy to previous stable config (rollback complete)
- Temporarily applied conservative rate limit for Solara's IP range to reduce retry amplification
- Forced warm KV cache for affected model family and pre-warmed two additional inference pools in us-west-2
- Enabled a circuit-breaker to shed excess requests and return fast failures to avoid queue pileups

Current status: error rate falling; we estimate 90% of affected requests have recovered; working on full catch-up for delayed requests. We will produce a detailed RCA and timeline in the next 48 hours and share mitigation artifacts.

Attachment: initial-mitigation-timeline.md (internal)

Hannah Schmitt
SRE Manager — Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

title: Post-close phased deployment milestones / MAP for OrchardCare
preview:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows

Hi Lydia —

Thanks again for the signature and trust — excited to get OrchardCare onto Redwood Dedicated. Attached is a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) that embeds the post-signature rollout, canary migration approach, and our training cadence. High-level items included:

- Week 0: Contract sign + SOW upload to DocuSign (we'll notify when countersigned)
- Week 1: Kickoff + environment security checklist + access handover
- Weeks 2–3: Canary deployment to 5% traffic, NV baseline + smoke tests
- Weeks 4–6: Full migration window, training sessions for product and ops teams (two 90-min workshops)
- Week 8: Go-live retrospective and hypercare runbook handoff

I've attached OrchardCare_MAP_v1.xlsx and the draft SOW (SOW_RW_OrchardCare.pdf). Please review the milestones and capacity windows and let me know constraints we should bake into the canary (e.g., blackout dates or maintenance windows).

Proposed kickoff: Thursday Aug 6, 10am PT — is that workable for your ops and security leads?

Thanks,
Irene

--
Irene Choi
Senior Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0129


From: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@orchardcare.com>, OrchardCare IT <it@orchardcare.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows

Irene —

Thanks for the clear plan. Quick notes after a fast scan:

- Aug 6 kickoff looks fine for leadership, but our clinical training team is out Aug 9–13 (annual conference). Can we avoid peak training in that week?
- For the canary: we need the canary to run across two facilities (NYC & Atlanta) to validate network egress under both carriers.
- Security would like the network-checklist.csv completed before Week 1; I attached the initial filled items (small edits).

I'll circulate to Tom and our security lead for a deeper review; we aim to get formal sign-off on the SOW by Fri Aug 6 so your proposed kickoff can remain intact. Please confirm you can extend the training window into Weeks 5–7 to avoid the Aug 9–13 blackout.

Also: are there prerequisites we need to complete for KMS integration? Our security team wants a short list in the MAP.

Best,
Lydia

--
Lydia Wells
Dir. Partnerships & Integrations | OrchardCare Health
lydia.wells@orchardcare.com


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Jul 2026 13:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows (OrchardCare)

Team — forwarding Lydia's note. Key asks we need to address:

1) Shift training out of Aug 9–13 blackout (move into Weeks 5–7).
2) Canary must cover two facilities (NYC & ATL) and include network egress profiling for each.
3) Provide KMS integration prerequisites in the MAP.
4) Complete and return network-checklist.csv (Lydia attached a filled-first-pass).

Marcus: can you confirm whether our canary traffic split supports multi-region facility targeting and what additional latency impact we should expect?
Priya: please draft a short KMS prerequisites list (3–5 items) and add to the MAP.
Sean: if we need any SRE support or an extended hypercare window, flag now.

We'll aim to produce OrchardCare_MAP_v1a with these updates and resend to Lydia by EOD Thu Jul 30.

Thanks —
Irene


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Jul 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows (OrchardCare)

Quick answers:

- Canary capability: yes — our traffic router can target traffic by origin facility (we tag by facility in the header). For two-facility canary we recommend split: 3% NYC, 2% ATL for an aggregate 5% and to limit blast radius.
- Expected delta: baseline latency increase of ~8–12ms p95 due to additional routing checks, but tokens-per-second unaffected. We'll include a short monitoring dashboard definition (p50/p95 token latency, error rate, dropped connections) in the MAP.
- Hypercare: recommend we offer a 2-week elevated SRE window post-go-live (Weeks 8–9) for quick rollback support; no charge for first week, second week at standard rate (per SOW terms).

I'll drop the monitoring bullet and the router notes into the MAP update.

Marcus
Senior Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@orchardcare.com>, OrchardCare IT <it@orchardcare.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 16:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Final MAP v1a and confirmed kickoff (kicking off Aug 6)

Lydia / team —

Attached: OrchardCare_MAP_v1a.xlsx (updated), SOW_RW_OrchardCare.pdf (refreshed), network-checklist.csv (completed). Changes since the draft:

- Training moved to Weeks 5–7 to avoid your Aug 9–13 conference blackout. New session dates are listed in the MAP (two product workshops: Aug 24 & Aug 26; two ops sessions: Aug 31 & Sept 2).
- Canary configuration updated to target NYC (3%) + ATL (2%) as Marcus recommended. Monitoring KPIs added (p50/p95 latency, error rate, token throughput).
- KMS prerequisites added (Priya): 1) KMS key ARN and IAM role with decrypt/encrypt permissions; 2) TLS mutual auth cert exchange process; 3) a test key and rotation window for validation — details in MAP.
- Hypercare: Redwood will provide 1 week complimentary elevated SRE coverage and an optional second week as paid support (details in SOW).

Confirming kickoff remains Thursday Aug 6, 10am PT — we'll send the calendar invite and a short agenda (kickoff objectives, roles/owners, immediate next steps). Please reply with any last-minute blockers; otherwise we will proceed with the kickoff and start Week 0 activities on Aug 7 (DocuSign countersign expected by then per your note).

Thanks and looking forward,
Irene

--

Subject: ACE onboarding request & PDM/PSM intros for DataQuarry opportunity

Hi Sara and team,

Quick one — we have a high-priority opportunity with DataQuarry (they're building an embedded analytics feature using LLMs). Customer is targeting an initial pilot in Q4 and asked us to pursue AWS co-sell / ACE registration to streamline procurement. Can you help kick off ACE registration and route to the right ACE PDM / PSM?

Deal quick context:
- Company: DataQuarry (Series B, healthcare analytics)
- Estimated monthly usage (pilot): ~250k tokens/day, burst to 1M tokens/day during trials
- Primary need: private VPC deployment + Dedicated capacity later
- Procurement ask: co-sell enablement + Marketplace listing to simplify purchase

Asks / next steps:
1) Start ACE registration for RWI-2026-045 and confirm any Marketplace SKU requirements.
2) Intro to the AWS ACE PDM and AWS PSM for the account (so we can align technical + procurement).
3) Marcus / Mina — please review the usage numbers and suggest an initial Dedicated sizing and rough SOW ballpark.

Attaching a short MAP and forecast for reference (DataQuarry-MAP.pdf). I'll follow up with the HubSpot link and our internal notes.

Thanks — appreciate the help here.

Best,
Sean Gallagher
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: DataQuarry-MAP.pdf (1 page, PDF)


From: Sara Brown <sara.brown@amazon.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>

- Do you plan to sell via Marketplace private offers or standard listing? (private offers can speed enterprise procurement).

Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: ACE onboarding request & PDM/PSM intros for DataQuarry opportunity

Thanks Sean — looping in Mina (our PSM lead) and dropping a quick sizing note. Mina — can you take point on aligning with the AWS PSM once Sara confirms the ACE assignment?

Sizing / delivery notes (very rough):
- Pilot: 1 g4dn.12xlarge equivalent Dedicated instance (for latency-sensitive evals) + autoscaling to handle bursts.
- KV/prefix cache will reduce steady-state tokens by ~25% for prompt-heavy workloads; plan for 2:1 reserved capacity buffer.
- Estimated monthly cost to customer (in our model): $18k-$30k depending on burst profile and caching.

I'm attaching a short capacity plan so the Marketplace and PDMs can see the numbers. We'll also prepare an SOW estimate if AWS needs an approximate procurement figure.

Hi all —

Thanks for the interest in a multi-prospect hands-on demo. Rather than separate one-offs, we thought a short \"timezone sprint\" of 3 x 45-minute cohort demos (US, EU, APAC) would let us cover core integrations and give each team focused time for Q&A.

Proposed cadence (all sessions are 45 minutes):
- US: Tue Jul 28, 11:00–11:45 PT
- EU: Wed Jul 29, 14:00–14:45 CEST
- APAC: Thu Jul 30, 09:00–09:45 SGT

Could each team confirm a slot or suggest alternatives by EOD Wed? We’ll finalize and send calendar invites.

title: SpectralAI: sudden retry surge causing 5xx/429s — credit request
preview:
From: Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: platform@spectralai.com
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production alert — widespread 5xx/429s and timeouts (July 14 08:40–09:05 PDT)

Irene / Redwood Support,

We experienced an outage window this morning where our integrations into your API started returning a mix of 500s and 429s beginning ~08:40 PDT and continuing in waves until ~09:05 PDT. Our telemetry shows a dramatic spike in client retries that overwhelmed our connection pool and caused downstream degradation. The attached logs include sample request traces and the Grafana snapshot of the error rate.

Impact: several of our end-user flows experienced timeouts; we had to circuit-break some calls to stop the cascade. We estimate this incident affected ~12% of live traffic and has direct revenue impact.

We are requesting confirmation of root cause, incident timeline, and a proposal for credits against July usage to cover the impact. Per our SLA we expect a reasonable credit for service unavailability and error-rate breaches.

Please advise next steps and who on your side will lead post-incident follow-up.

Thanks,
Alex Mercer
Head of Platform Reliability
SpectralAI

[Attachment: error_log_20260714.txt (text/plain)]
[Attachment: grafana_20260714_spike.png (image/png)]

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production alert — widespread 5xx/429s and timeouts (July 14 08:40–09:05 PDT)

Alex — thanks for the quick heads-up and the logs. I’m very sorry for the disruption this morning. We’re treating this as an elevated incident and I’ve looped in engineering and sales so we can (1) get you a technical post-mortem, (2) propose a credit/remediation offer, and (3) coordinate any operational mitigations you need while we investigate.

Dev — can you ingest Alex's logs and flag any immediately actionable mitigations (rate limiting, circuit-breaker guidance, temporary routing) while we investigate the root cause? Sean / I will handle the commercial side and confirm an initial credit offer so SpectralAI can reconcile this quickly.

Alex — we’ll aim to come back within 24 hours with both a preliminary cause and a credit proposal.

Irene Choi
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference

[Inline: forwarded attachments from SpectralAI]


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production alert — widespread 5xx/429s and timeouts (July 14 08:40–09:05 PDT)

I dug into the traces Alex attached and our side's objective telemetry. Quick summary:
- Observed a sudden client-side retry pattern from a small set of SpectralAI request IPs beginning 08:38 PDT. The client retried on 5xx responses with an aggressive backoff (linear, very short delay), causing many near-simultaneous reconnects.
- Our API gateway saw a burst of identical request fingerprints; the continuous batching runtime attempted to allocate KV cache and ephemeral worker slots, and under the surge we returned 503s while queueing hit hard limits. There were also some 429s from our rate-limiter as protection kicked in.

Immediate mitigations we recommend while we complete the RCA:
1) Temporary soft rate-limit on the SpectralAI key to smooth bursts (we can apply a 20% smoothing window for 2 hours).
2) Client-side guidance: increase retry backoff, jitter, and cap max attempts. We can share a recommended SDK patch.
3) We will temporarily increase worker pool on the route for the next 6 hours to reduce tail errors.

I think this is primarily an amplification problem: client retries -> server-side queue pressure -> 5xx/429s -> more retries. We'll provide a full timeline and root cause within ~8 business hours.

- Dev
Principal Software Engineer, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: spectral_request_trace.json (application/json) — sanitized request IDs included]

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>, Julia Park <cfo@spectralai.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 11:16:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed remediation credit for July 14 incident — SpectralAI (DEAL-8421)

Alex / Julia — thanks again for raising this and for your patience while we validated the root cause. Per Dev’s findings, the incident was a retry-amplification event exacerbated by aggressive client retries. We accept partial operational responsibility for not having earlier smoothing thresholds that could have absorbed the spike.

Commercial proposal: Redwood proposes a one-time credit equal to 25% of SpectralAI's July usage invoice, applied as a billing credit to your account, contingent on the acceptance of the attached incident report and a short mutual action plan that adds explicit client-side retry guidance to the integration docs. Rationale: we recognize the impact and want to make SpectralAI whole for the immediate bill, while also aligning on fixes that reduce recurrence risk.

If this is acceptable we can (a) issue the credit immediately and reflect it in the next billing cycle, or (b) if you prefer an immediate cash refund we can route through finance (would take up to 7 business days). If you need an escalated review for a larger remediation figure, please let us know and we'll route to the executive committee.

Next steps I propose:
- SpectralAI confirms preferred remediation (billing credit vs refund) and whether 25% is acceptable.
- We sign/MOU the mutual action plan (2–3 bullets) and timeframe.
- Redwood implements the temporary smoothing and shares the SDK guidance within 24 hours.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Redwood_INCIDENT_20260714_report.pdf (summary, omitted in inline)]

From: Julia Park <cfo@spectralai.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 18:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed remediation credit for July 14 incident — SpectralAI (DEAL-8421)

Sean / Irene — thank you for the quick investigation and the proposed plan. We appreciate Redwood recognizing the impact. Given the measurable revenue and customer complaints we incurred during the window, 25% of July usage is lower than we expected. Julia's ask: provide an alternate proposal of 40% credit or a phased option — 25% immediate credit plus a discretionary uplift after the post-incident review if the RCA shows missed mitigations on Redwood's side.

We are open to signing the mutual action plan quickly but need a stronger initial remediation to cover marketing and support costs incurred. Please escalate for an exec decision and let us know if Redwood prefers the billing-credit path vs. direct refund.

Regards,
Julia Park
CFO, SpectralAI

[Inline: quote of Sean's 25% proposal]

To: Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>, Julia Park <cfo@spectralai.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 11:16:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed remediation credit for July 14 incident — SpectralAI (DEAL-8421)

Alex / Julia — thanks again for raising this and for your patience while we validated the root cause. Per Dev’s findings, the incident was a retry-amplification event exacerbated by aggressive client retries. We accept partial operational responsibility for not having earlier smoothing thresholds that could have absorbed the spike.

Commercial proposal: Redwood proposes a one-time credit equal to 25% of SpectralAI's July usage invoice, applied as a billing credit to your account, contingent on the acceptance of the attached incident report and a short mutual action plan that adds explicit client-side retry guidance to the integration docs. Rationale: we recognize the impact and want to make SpectralAI whole for the immediate bill, while also aligning on fixes that reduce recurrence risk.

If this is acceptable we can (a) issue the credit immediately and reflect it in the next billing cycle, or (b) if you prefer an immediate cash refund we can route through finance (would take up to 7 business days). If you need an escalated review for a larger remediation figure, please let us know and we'll route to the executive committee.

Next steps I propose:
- SpectralAI confirms preferred remediation (billing credit vs refund) and whether 25% is acceptable.
- We sign/MOU the mutual action plan (2–3 bullets) and timeframe.
- Redwood implements the temporary smoothing and shares the SDK guidance within 24 hours.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Redwood_INCIDENT_20260714_report.pdf (summary, omitted in inline)]

From: Julia Park <cfo@spectralai.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Alex Mercer <ops@spectralai.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 18:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposed remediation credit for July 14 incident — SpectralAI (DEAL-8421)

Sean / Irene — thank you for the quick investigation and the proposed plan. We appreciate Redwood recognizing the impact. Given the measurable revenue and customer complaints we incurred during the window, 25% of July usage is lower than we expected. Julia's ask: provide an alternate proposal of 40% credit or a phased option — 25% immediate credit plus a discretionary uplift after the post-incident review if the RCA shows missed mitigations on Redwood's side.

We are open to signing the mutual action plan quickly but need a stronger initial remediation to cover marketing and support costs incurred. Please escalate for an exec decision and let us know if Redwood prefers the billing-credit path vs. direct refund.

Regards,
Julia Park
CFO, SpectralAI

[Inline: quote of Sean's 25% proposal]

title: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T08:14:00Z
Subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Team —

Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts. Key asks from Novus:
- 2x nominal RPS for first 48 hours (expected peak: 220 RPS, baseline 110)
- Temporary increase to token throughput for streaming requests (peak ~1.6M tokens/min)
- Warmstart KV cache retention for conversation sessions (they expect 10–15s between follow-up calls)

I need us to confirm feasibility and the operational checklist: entitlements from Billing, IP whitelist, route tag + canary rollout, and an emergency throttling rollback plan. Priya — what product toggles do we need to enable for warmstart retention and fallback model variant? Karthik — can infra support a 2x uplift for 48 hours with a safe drain plan? Marcus/Evelyn — any telemetry/alerting we should pre-configure in Console?

I've attached their forecast spreadsheet and the entitlement request they emailed. I'll loop in Novus after we agree on the internal playbook.

Thanks,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: novus_warmstart_forecast.xlsx (spreadsheet), entitlement_request_novus.pdf (PDF)


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement:

1) Warmstart KV semantics: we can increase per-session KV retention to 60s for the pilot but it will impact memory usage on the serving pool. We should scope which routes actually need this — recommend enabling for the /chat/stream route only.

2) Fallback model variant: we can prepare a quantized 8-bit variant (same architecture) that reduces GPU token cost ~40%. We need ~24 hours to validate quality on their smoke prompts and to add the variant to the routing policy.

3) Throttle window & quotas: product can support a timebound ephemeral entitlement (48 hours max) that maps to a temp rate limit and a post-window automatic rollback. We should specify exact start/end UTC times in the entitlement doc.

Questions:
- Expected percent of requests that are streaming vs non-streaming? The forecast lists tokens but not streaming mix.
- Do they plan long-running streaming sessions (>30s)? That changes KV and connection resource planning.

If we get answers by EOD we can ship the fallback model and the entitlement change in time.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Inference

On 2026-07-13 08:14, Avery Johnson wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: 

- Pool capacity: current dedicated pool for pilot-grade tenants has ~30% spare headroom; pushing to 2x will require borrowing from the shared burst pool. That is feasible but we need an explicit borrow-and-return schedule to avoid impacting other pilots.

- KV cache retention: increasing retention to 60s is ok for the /chat/stream route only. We'll need to set per-route cache limits (max entries per session) — recommend max 256 tokens stored per session as a guardrail.

- Canary & rollback: We'll publish a route tag (novus-pilot-48h) and do a canary rollout at 10% for the first hour. If error rate or 95p latency increases beyond 2x baseline we'll auto-roll back. We need Product to sign off on the rollback SLA thresholds.

- Whitelisting: Novus must provide egress IP ranges for their client traffic (or we can accept a JWT assertion). Marcus — please coordinate with Network for the 2–3 CIDR blocks.

I'll push a runbook for ops and a short playbook for on-call to the shared drive once we confirm times.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineering / Infra

On 2026-07-13 09:02, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement...

Cc: CloudOps <cloudops@novus.ai>, Procurement <procurement@novus.ai>
Date: 2026-07-13T13:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Hi Avery —

Thanks for coordinating. Quick answers from our side: 
- Streaming vs non-streaming: ~70% streaming (short bursts), 30% normal request/response. Median session ~12s, 95p ~45s.
- Ramp schedule: 2026-07-18 02:00–08:00 UTC (nights), 2026-07-19 scaled ramp 00:00–10:00 UTC. We'll provide a CSV with our expected IP egress ranges by EOD today.
- Procurement will review the entitlement doc; they asked whether we can get a one-time credit if we exceed the uptick during the pilot window (they want a safety net).

We appreciate the fallback variant offer — would like the 8-bit option on standby.

Best,
Alice Park
Head of Product, Novus AI

I'll reply-all to Novus once we have confirmed the whitelist CIDRs and Sean confirms the entitlement doc.

Avery

title: Renewal pipeline hygiene: Q3 commit verification & risk buckets
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodai.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwoodai.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodai.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@sean_gallagher@redwoodai.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 08:02:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal pipeline hygiene: Q3 commit verification & risk buckets

Team —

As discussed on yesterday's ops sync, finance needs a cleaned up Q3 renewal commit by EOD Monday so forecasting is ready for the July board packet. I attached a working sheet (q3_renewal_hygiene_slate.xlsx) with the current set of accounts we list as \"Commit\" / \"Best Case\" / \"At Risk.\" Please do the following before EOD Monday: 

- Mark each account with a risk bucket: GREEN / YELLOW / RED and add one short reason (30 chars max).
- For any RED accounts, add the specific blocker (procurement, budget, security, product, other) and the next owner for outreach.
- Confirm committed ARR number in the sheet matches HubSpot (link in related_links). Vivek, please flag any accounting gotchas if the ARR number includes prepaid credits.
- If you have an active contract redline, attach or point to it in the grid.

Focus note: Nimbus Health is showing up as a semi-commit in the CRM but we have outstanding SOC2 evidence and an outstanding ELS pricing ask. If we can't move that to GREEN, assume it as a 60% probability for the forecast until we have a signed doc.

I'll do a quick follow-up on Monday morning to capture final commits and roll to finance. Thanks — tight turnaround on this one.

Jordan
Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwoodai.com
(415) 555-0143
