Subject: Re: Renewal pipeline hygiene: Q3 commit verification & risk buckets

Jordan —

I've reviewed my book and updated the spreadsheet with initial risk buckets for the 7 accounts I own. Quick highlights: 

- Nimbus Health: MARKED YELLOW -> moved to RED in a comment because legal is waiting on our SOC2 attestation and they pushed for a 12-month paid case study discount. Outreach owner: Sean. Next step: schedule a technical readout and share SOC2 evidence.
- Acme Health: GREEN -> finance confirmed ARR matches HubSpot. Maria at Acme is ok with our renewal terms, just confirming PO timing (I included her email in the sheet).
- Meridian Labs: YELLOW -> procurement asked for a month extension; likely stays in Brownstein (my flag) until updated POC signoff.

I attached the updated sheet and included links to the HubSpot deals for each row. I'll keep chasing, will flag anything bumped to RED by Monday morning.

—Monica

Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Renewal pipeline hygiene: Q3 commit verification & risk buckets

1) If a customer is flagged as \"Commit\" but their renewal contains a non-recurring credit (one-time onboarding or marketing credit), we should push the ARR cell to a separate column \"commit_recurring_arr\" so forecast math isn't overstated. Monica — I see Acme has a $30k TOV credit recorded; please move that to the \"non-recurring\" column.

2) For Nimbus (DEAL-4821), accounting shows the contract value on HubSpot as $420k ARR but invoicing terms proposed are 6-month prepay. If they request an invoice schedule change, probability math needs a manual note.

I'll add a finance tab to the sheet with a reconciliation column. Also - can someone paste the HubSpot deal link into the related_links so FP&A can validate?

title: MAP: performance validation plan and emb + rerank load verification
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Hi Elena / Carlos —

Attaching an initial Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and a proposed performance validation plan for the embeddings + reranker endpoints. High-level asks:

- Confirm dataset and tokenization expectations (we need a representative query set, sample size ~12k queries).
- Confirm test windows on your side (we'll need a 4-hour window for the full concurrency sweep).
- Agree targets: 95th p99 latency under 350ms for embeddings (per vector), reranker TTFT p95 < 700ms @ 8 concurrent requests, and sustained throughput targets (tokens/s).

What we've attached: searchly_map_perf_plan.xlsx (MAP + timeline + responsibilities), and a short test harness note. The plan includes an incremental approach: smoke -> controlled concurrency sweep -> sustained 1-hour soak. We will run on Redwood Dedicated test pool that mirrors your prod-ish GPU type (A100 equivalent).

If you confirm the dataset and a tentative test window next week we can lock the schedule and execute a P0 run. Goal from Sales side: have performance artifacts for internal sign-off and for procurement by 2026-07-08.

Thanks —
Karthik
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference

Attachment: searchly_map_perf_plan.xlsx (MAP and perf plan)


From: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Thanks Karthik — this looks good as a starting point. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Dataset: we'll produce the 12k query bundle (searchly_test_dataset_v3.zip) and drop a link into the shared doc by Tuesday EOD. It contains anonymized user queries + groundtruth relevancy labels for the reranker.
2) Parity: we need to confirm that the Dedicated test pool uses the same sequence length caps as our prod model (max 1024 tokens) — can you confirm?
3) Concurrency: happy to run the 4-hour window; please include an extra run at 50 concurrent sessions for peak tail behavior.

We can aim for the test window on Wed 24th. CC'ing Carlos to confirm procurement timelines.

—Elena
Product Ops, Searchly
elena@searchly.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 14:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Elena — quick logistics from the bench team:

- Environment: Redwood Dedicated (test pool) is configured with NVidia A100-s equivalent, sequence length cap set to 1024 (confirmed). Tokenizer parity: we'll use the same tokenizer binary you provide.
- Harness: running redwood-bench v2.1 + our JMeter wrapper (emb_rerank_load_script.jmx attached). The harness records latency (p50/p95/p99), TTFT (time to first token for rerank streaming), per-request tokens generated, and throughput (tokens/sec).
- Test matrix (summary):
  * Smoke: 5 concurrent -> verify correctness
  * Sweep: 1,2,4,8,16,32,50 concurrency (ramp 1m steps)
  * Soak: 60 minutes at target concurrency (16 and 50 runs)

Targets we recommend (for MAP acceptance):
- Embeddings: median < 40ms, p95 < 120ms
- Reranker (single-pass, no external sidecars): TTFT p50 < 220ms, p95 < 700ms; sustained throughput >= 140 requests/min at 8 concurrency (proxy for production QPS).

I'll run a smoke on Tuesday morning and share baseline artifacts (logs + CSV). If anything looks off we can re-run with tuned batching/quantization flags.

-Marissa
Perf Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: emb_rerank_load_script.jmx


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 11:47:00 -0700
Subject: Interim run results: emb + rerank (summary)

Elena / team — ran the controlled sweep this morning (dataset subset ~3k queries) and attached the CSV with raw samples (emb_rerank_load_report.csv). High-level summary below:

Embeddings endpoint (single-vector calls)\\- Concurrency 1: p50 18ms, p95 42ms, throughput 750 vectors/s\\- Concurrency 8: p50 22ms, p95 110ms, throughput 5.6k vectors/s\\- Concurrency 50: p50 48ms, p95 190ms, throughput 18.2k vectors/s

Reranker endpoint (ranker returns top-K, streaming first token)\\- Concurrency 1: TTFT p50 140ms, p95 295ms, throughput 18 req/s\\- Concurrency 8: TTFT p50 210ms, p95 610ms, throughput 128 req/min\\- Concurrency 50: TTFT p50 420ms, p95 1320ms, throughput 720 req/hr

Notes:
- The 50-concurrency run shows p95 degradation for reranker (1.3s). This looks related to CPU-bound postprocessing after the model pass (we're capturing host metrics). We can mitigate via a short-term worker scaling tweak in the mapping, or use an optimized postproc binary.
- Embeddings stayed well within MAP targets up to 32 conc. At 50 we see higher p95 but median remains acceptable.

Next steps suggested:

2) Full soak (60m) at 16 concurrency to verify stability.
3) Final artifacts + MAP update for sales sign-off.

Subject: Re: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Elena — quick logistics from the bench team:

- Environment: Redwood Dedicated (test pool) is configured with NVidia A100-s equivalent, sequence length cap set to 1024 (confirmed). Tokenizer parity: we'll use the same tokenizer binary you provide.
- Harness: running redwood-bench v2.1 + our JMeter wrapper (emb_rerank_load_script.jmx attached). The harness records latency (p50/p95/p99), TTFT (time to first token for rerank streaming), per-request tokens generated, and throughput (tokens/sec).
- Test matrix (summary):
  * Smoke: 5 concurrent -> verify correctness
  * Sweep: 1,2,4,8,16,32,50 concurrency (ramp 1m steps)
  * Soak: 60 minutes at target concurrency (16 and 50 runs)

Targets we recommend (for MAP acceptance):
- Embeddings: median < 40ms, p95 < 120ms
- Reranker (single-pass, no external sidecars): TTFT p50 < 220ms, p95 < 700ms; sustained throughput >= 140 requests/min at 8 concurrency (proxy for production QPS).

I'll run a smoke on Tuesday morning and share baseline artifacts (logs + CSV). If anything looks off we can re-run with tuned batching/quantization flags.

-Marissa
Perf Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: emb_rerank_load_script.jmx

title: Executive recap: outcomes, constraints, and escalation slate for NovaNet discussion
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
To: Julia Park <julia.park@novanet.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-06-24T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Executive recap: outcomes, constraints, and escalation slate for NovaNet discussion

Julia — thanks again for your time earlier today. Quick recap from our discussion with the leadership team and the asks I captured. I wanted to make sure we’re aligned on the core outcomes, the constraints that will drive procurement, and the potential escalation points so we can draft a clean mutual action plan.

High-level business outcomes you called out
- Reduce inference tail latency that spikes during batch ingestion windows — target: p50 <200ms, p95 <500ms for the search-rewrite pipeline.
- Stop the month-over-month cost drift on embedding generation; objective is ~30% unit-cost reduction for embeddings by Q4 through batching/quantization.
- Maintain full auditability and tenant isolation for regulated traffic; SOC 2 + VPC controls are non-negotiable.

Primary constraints / risks (leadership lens)
- Procurement will require an ops + security checklist (SOC 2 attestation, network diagrams, KMS integration) before legal will sign.
- Data residency requirement for EU traffic; NovaNet needs a clear region routing policy and proof of in-region KV/cache behavior.
- CFO will push hard on a predictable cost model — they won’t accept open-ended usage exposure for pilot.

Immediate asks / next steps
1) I’ll send a draft mutual action plan (MAP) with milestones for POC → pilot → procurement (attached). Please review and flag missing stakeholders.
2) Marissa / Sean to schedule a technical deep-dive next week with your platform team to validate latency and KV/cache expectations.
3) We’ll share SOC 2 artifacts and our private deployment architecture diagram (attached).
4) Julia to confirm the procurement gatekeepers (legal, procurement, CFO point of contact) and an anticipated approval window — you mentioned early Q4 as a target.

Attachments referenced: MAP-draft.xlsx, inference-cost-model.csv, redwood-architecture-v1.pdf

If that captures things accurately, we’ll convert the MAP into a timeline and surface any gating items to your procurement lead. Happy to jump on a quick sync to walk through the attachments.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.inference | +1 415-555-0164

Also adding a gating item: NovaNet’s CFO needs an explicit cap for pilot spend (our standard approach is a capped pilot credit + overage alerting). I’ll propose a pilot cap in the MAP and ask them to confirm.

Sean — can you pick 3 slots next week for the platform deep-dive (60–75 min)? I’ll draft the security checklist to append to the MAP.

Thanks — Marissa

Karthik / Marissa — thank you for the concise recap. That aligns with our internal readout. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Decision makers: CTO (Ravi Shah) and CFO (Anita Desai) will have final sign-off — Ravi will validate architecture and latency behavior, Anita will sign off on the commercial terms and pilot cap.
- Timeline: we’re aiming to complete vendor shortlisting by the end of August and select a pilot partner in early September. Procurement expects a 6–8 week legal review after pilot success.

Pilot pricing / cap: yes, we support a capped pilot model. Typical structure we propose: a fixed pilot credit (we can start at $25k) covering X tokens / Y embedding units + an agreed overage alerting threshold and rate card. I’ll put a pilot cap option in the MAP and present two tiered caps for your CFO to consider.

Security artifacts: attached redwood-architecture-v1.pdf; we’ll provide a region routing note for EU traffic and a KMS integration brief. Priya will deliver the SOC 2 snapshot and a short FAQ on log retention/access controls at the technical deep-dive.

POC acceptance criteria (to be included in MAP):
- Latency: p50 <200ms and p95 <500ms under representative load (TBD test harness)
- Cost: unit embedding cost reduced by >=25% during pilot measured on matched workload
- Accuracy: no >3% regression on current semantic search baseline (we’ll propose evaluation prompts)

Next steps:
- Marissa to circulate 3 deep-dive slots for 6/30 — please confirm availability.
- I’ll update the MAP (attached) with pilot cap options and an explicit POC acceptance section and push to HubSpot: NN-2026-0042.
- If pilot acceptance criteria are acceptable to NovaNet leadership, we’ll prepare a short SOW and legal checklist for initial sign-off.

Links: HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deals/2048/NN-2026-0042), DocuSign PO template (https://www.docusign.com/link/NN-0042-POC)

Thanks — will follow up with the MAP update by end of day.

Best,

Redwood Inference

title: Urgent: Regional service degradation impacting Acme — routing & remediation
preview:
From: Natalie Cho <natalie.cho@acme-corp.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Elevated errors and latency in us-west-2 — urgent help needed

Hi Karthik,

We're seeing a sustained problem starting ~08:50 PT this morning in our production service talking to Redwood. Symptoms:
- 5xx error rate ~12% for generation endpoints (spiky 502/504)
- p95 latency ~900ms, p99 ~1.8s (previously p99 ~320ms)
- throughput drop ~30% for our steady-state load (token inflight queues growing)

We rely on the us-west-2 deployment for most traffic. Can you escalate? Our incident is impacting several customers and internal SLAs. Attaching a short snippets file with request/response headers and timestamps.

We need confirmation of: 1) whether this is an AZ-level issue, 2) any immediate mitigations (routing/fallback) we can enable, and 3) expected timeline.

Thanks,
Natalie Cho
Platform Engineering — Acme Corp
attachments: acme-error-snippets.log


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Natalie Cho <natalie.cho@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-06-20T09:24:31-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Elevated errors and latency in us-west-2 — urgent help needed

Folks — forwarding an escalation from Acme (prod is suffering). Key points from their telemetry above. Natalie, logging the thread here so we can coordinate.

Initial hypotheses (from looking at our dashboards):
- Multiple compute nodes in us-west-2 AZ-a reported terminations and fast reattachments ~08:45–09:00. That matches the timing.
- KV cache miss rate jumped (prefix cache evictions during rolling resizes) which increases decode latency for warm generators.
- NLB 502 spikes correlated with a small subset of inference workers that ran out of GPU memory and restarted.

Immediate asks:
1) Ops (Sean/Dev) — can you propose a 1-hour remediation plan we can execute now?
2) Monica — can you validate whether Acme's traffic is tagged as PII/high-sensitivity so we can decide on cross-region fallback?
3) I'll draft a short external message to Acme once we have the remediation decisions.

I'm attaching a quick logs summary I pulled from the monitoring cluster.

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Exec — Redwood Inference
attachments: rz-logs-summary-0620.csv


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Natalie Cho <natalie.cho@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-06-20T09:33:10-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Elevated errors and latency in us-west-2 — urgent help needed

Karthik / team — quick update from monitoring correlates:
- Instance termination flood primarily in AZ-a of us-west-2 between 08:42–08:58. AZ-b/c nodes are largely healthy but we see elevated queue depth.
- Token queue depth (global) increased by ~3.2x; some older requests hit cold KV cache -> longer tail latency.

Recommendation checklist (short):
- Short term (10–20m): implement routing policy to prefer healthy AZ-b/c within us-west-2 and add a forced fallback to eu-west-1 only for non-PII routes. We must get Acme signoff on cross-region for sensitive data.
- Short term (10–30m): scale up dedicated pool in us-west-2 by +2 GPU nodes and increase min_autoscale to avoid rapid scale-down during recovery.
- Short-medium (30–90m): roll a soft restart of the affected worker group with a changed kernel / memory cap to reduce OOM restarts.

I can coordinate with networking to push the routing policy change if ops approves. Natalie — can you confirm whether cross-region to eu-west-1 is acceptable for anything not explicitly marked PII?

-Monica
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-20T09:52:08-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Elevated errors and latency in us-west-2 — remediation play

Team — proposed play (I will own ops actions). Timed plan below (all times PT):

T+0m (09:52):
- Push routing override to blueprint: new policy set -> prefer us-west-2 AZ-b/c; if p50 healthy < 300ms in AZ-b/c accept; else route to eu-west-1 for non-PII. (See runbook link: https://drive.redwood.ai/drive/folders/teams/ops/INC-2026-06-20)
- Increase dedicated pool in us-west-2: request +2 A100 nodes (est 6–8 min to spin).

T+10m (10:02):
- Enable temporary model-variant fallback (quantized-8bit variant) for bulk requests for Acme to reduce memory pressure and lower OOM restarts; flag for degradation in QA.
- Set autoscale min nodes to current+1 to avoid immediate downscales during resync.

T+30m (10:22):
- Recycle affected worker group with tightened memory cap and updated kernel selection. Monitor 5xx and p99.

Communication: Karthik to send an external note to Acme with the plan and expected windows. I'll post status updates in the incident folder and ping infra on Slack.

I'll attach the quick AZ node map and the runbook.

-Sean
Head of Site Reliability — Redwood Inference
attachments: rw-az-node-map.png, runbook-cross-region-fallback.pdf


From: Natalie Cho <natalie.cho@acme-corp.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-20T10:43:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Urgent: Regional service degradation impacting Acme — routing & remediation

Karthik / team — thanks for the rapid response. We agreed internally that non-PII traffic can be routed to eu-west-1 while this stabilizes.

Current status (10:40 PT):
- 5xx error rate dropped to ~0.8%
- p99 latency improved to ~420ms
- Throughput recovered to ~95% of baseline

We see no user-visible incidents continuing; we appreciate the transparency. Please keep the routing override and capacity bump in place until telemetry is stable for at least 2 hours, and then we can stage traffic back.

Natalie
Platform Engineering — Acme Corp

title: On-prem tiers, billing runway and migration credit — Acme RFP
preview:
From: Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com, Sara Lim <sara.lim@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Ava —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is a short excerpt from our RFP (Acme_RFP_2026.pdf). Two asks from our procurement + infra teams:

1) We need a commercial summary that separates the one-time on-prem license (if any), the recurring platform fee (monthly/annual), and estimated per-token or per-inference metering. Our finance lead needs line items for capex vs opex justification.

2) We're particularly interested in understanding whether Redwood will consider a migration / onboarding credit to offset professional services and initial GPU churn. If you have a simple rule (e.g., X% credit up to $Y with a minimum commit), share it.

Timing: Acme's fiscal Q3 starts July 1; procurement is trying to lock a contract by 7/15 if possible.

Couple of clarifying items in the RFP: we plan to run a private VPC deployment with a phased rollout (pilot -> prod). We expect 24/7 tier-2/oncall coverage during pilot and a higher SLA for prod.

Can you send a short commercial (one page) and a suggested support-tier mapping we can circulate?

Thanks —
Tom
Tom Blake
Director, Platform Engineering | Acme Health

Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial and a support-tier mapping and get that to you by EOD Friday. Quick questions while I assemble:

- Do you prefer pricing presented as (A) annual committed spend with an amortized platform fee, or (B) metered + smaller platform fee? We can show both.
- Is the pilot chargeable under the same commit as prod, or do you want a separate pilot allowance?
- Any constraint on invoice cadence (monthly vs quarterly vs annual)? You mentioned FY start — we can align billing to July if a signature happens before 7/15.

Best,
Ava Chen

Team —

Forwarding Acme's RFP excerpt and Tom's note. They want: a one-page commercial separating license / platform fee / metering, and a clear migration/onboarding credit rule. Target sign by 7/15 if we want to align with their FY.

Quick requirements pulled from the RFP: pilot -> prod phased rollout; 24/7 tier-2 coverage during pilot; stricter SLA for prod; likely GCP us-west region for the pilot.

Suggested asks from our side that we should confirm:
- Commit bands: $250k, $500k, $750k annual committed spend — each with tiered discounts to list price.
- Platform fee: option to amortize an annual platform fee ($X) into the monthly invoices vs. invoice as a single annual line.
- Migration credit: allow up to 25% of onboarding fees back as a credit applied to the first 3 invoices if customer commits >= $750k. Alternatively, a fixed $Y credit if they pick annual prepay.

What I need from you:
- Rachel: recommended discount bands and redlines we should avoid (e.g., no unlimited credits).
- Vivek: support-tier definitions and rough $ for managed services during pilot vs prod (PS days and runbook onboarding).
- Sean: finance/booking implications — if we amortize platform fee over 12 months vs bill upfront, what's the preferred booking and any revenue recognition flags?

Attachments: acme_rfp_excerpt.txt (text, 4KB)

Thanks —
Ava

> On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 10:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Tom —
> > 
> > Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial...


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Folks —

Thanks for the context. Below is a pragmatic commercial structure that balances Acme's capex/opex asks and our unit economics. Numbers are rough — I can refine if you want a playbook table.

1) Commitment bands (annual committed spend):
   - Band A: $250k/year — list discounts: 10% off hosted list; platform fee $40k/year (or $3.3k/mo amortized).
   - Band B: $500k/year — list discounts: 18%; platform fee $30k/year (or $2.5k/mo amortized).
   - Band C: $750k+/year — list discounts: 25%; platform fee $20k/year (or $1.7k/mo amortized).

2) Pilot treatment:
   - Offer a 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit that does not count against the annual commit if they sign within 45 days of pilot close. This helps remove friction and keeps pilot from eating into committed capacity.

3) Migration / onboarding credit:
   - Conditional credit: up to 25% of professional services fees (capped at $120k) applied as an invoice credit over first 3 monthly invoices if customer signs Band C (>= $750k).
   - Alternative: $50k one-time onboarding credit if customer prepays annual fee.

4) Support tiers (recommended mapping):
   - Standard (included): business hours support, 48-hr SLA for severity 1
   - Premium: 24/7 monitoring + 4-hr response for sev1, dedicated CS engineer (adds $6k/mo)
   - Enterprise: 24/7 on-call with guaranteed on-site/remote runbook triage within 2 hours, uptime SLA 99.95% (adds $13k/mo + optional oncall retainer)

5) Billing / booking notes (Sean):
   - We can amortize the platform fee for invoicing purposes; Finance should be okay as long as we have a signed SOW capturing the amortization schedule. If they insist on a single upfront platform fee, expect different revenue recognition.

6) Holdbacks / redlines to avoid:
   - No unlimited credits or open-ended PS estimates. Credits must be conditional on commit or prepay.
   - Keep on-call response SLAs tied to the support tier purchase.

If that sounds good, I'll draft a short one-pager and a recommended clause for the PS credit.

-Vivek
VP, Solutions & Partnerships | Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@acmehealth.com
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 11:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Tom —

Thanks for the detail and timeline. Per our conversation and internal alignment, attached is a concise commercial summary and proposed support mapping for the Acme pilot -> prod path. Highlights below:

- Commitment bands (annual committed spend): Band A $250k (10% off hosted list), Band B $500k (18%), Band C $750k+ (25%).
- Platform fee: option to amortize over 12 months (we'll reflect both amortized and upfront invoice rows in the attached PDF).
- Pilot: 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit. Pilot credit will be waived from the commit if contract is executed within 45 days of pilot completion.
- Migration / onboarding credit: conditional credit up to 25% of PS fees (capped at $120k) applied across the first three invoices if Acme signs Band C (>= $750k). Alternatively, we can offer a $50k one-time credit for annual prepay.
- Support: Standard (included), Premium (+$6k/mo), Enterprise (+$13k/mo + optional oncall retainer). Premium meets the 24/7 monitoring ask during pilot; Enterprise offers the tighter SLA for prod.

Next steps: If this structure looks reasonable we will:
1) Produce a short SOW + pricing worksheet (attached) and a DocuSign-ready commercial.
2) Engage Solutions (Vivek) for PS scoping call next week — propose Tue 6/23 at 10am PT.
3) Align invoicing cadence — we can invoice monthly and amortize the platform fee, or invoice platform fee annually per your preference.

I've attached Redwood_Proposal_ACME_0619.pdf with the one-pager and two billing scenarios. If you want any adjustments before we produce a DocuSign, flag them and I'll update.

Cheers —
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Strategic & Enterprise | Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 415-555-0189

Folks —

Thanks for the context. Below is a pragmatic commercial structure that balances Acme's capex/opex asks and our unit economics. Numbers are rough — I can refine if you want a playbook table.

1) Commitment bands (annual committed spend):
   - Band A: $250k/year — list discounts: 10% off hosted list; platform fee $40k/year (or $3.3k/mo amortized).
   - Band B: $500k/year — list discounts: 18%; platform fee $30k/year (or $2.5k/mo amortized).
   - Band C: $750k+/year — list discounts: 25%; platform fee $20k/year (or $1.7k/mo amortized).

2) Pilot treatment:
   - Offer a 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit that does not count against the annual commit if they sign within 45 days of pilot close. This helps remove friction and keeps pilot from eating into committed capacity.

3) Migration / onboarding credit:
   - Conditional credit: up to 25% of professional services fees (capped at $120k) applied as an invoice credit over first 3 monthly invoices if customer signs Band C (>= $750k).
   - Alternative: $50k one-time onboarding credit if customer prepays annual fee.

4) Support tiers (recommended mapping):
   - Standard (included): business hours support, 48-hr SLA for severity 1
   - Premium: 24/7 monitoring + 4-hr response for sev1, dedicated CS engineer (adds $6k/mo)
   - Enterprise: 24/7 on-call with guaranteed on-site/remote runbook triage within 2 hours, uptime SLA 99.95% (adds $13k/mo + optional oncall retainer)

5) Billing / booking notes (Sean):
   - We can amortize the platform fee for invoicing purposes; Finance should be okay as long as we have a signed SOW capturing the amortization schedule. If they insist on a single upfront platform fee, expect different revenue recognition.

6) Holdbacks / redlines to avoid:
   - No unlimited credits or open-ended PS estimates. Credits must be conditional on commit or prepay.
   - Keep on-call response SLAs tied to the support tier purchase.

If that sounds good, I'll draft a short one-pager and a recommended clause for the PS credit.

-Vivek
VP, Solutions & Partnerships | Redwood Inference

title: Approval needed: NewCo 3yr price-stability & renewal protection
preview:
From: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@newco.ai
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Hi Aditya / Laura,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the team was impressed with the latency and routing controls. We're preparing a recommended vendor list for our platform consolidation and would like to propose a 3-year agreement with two commercial asks:

- A firm price lock for the first 24 months, with a capped uplift of +5% for years 3 (not to exceed +8% over the entire term).
- A renewal-protection clause: in the event NewCo is acquired or changes cloud region, we need the option to renew at the same unit economics or a pre-agreed formula.

Attached is the RFP summary and our standard procurement template. Can you confirm if Redwood can accept these two asks and whether legal will accept the renewal language? We have a June 25 procurement board meeting that we are targeting.

Best,
Caroline
Caroline Hsu
Head of Procurement, NewCo
---
Attachment: NewCo_RFP_summary.pdf (pdf, 312 KB)

----
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Caroline —

Appreciate the clear asks and the timeline. Short answer: we can support a 24-month fixed-price window and a capped uplift; the renewal-protection clause is policy-dependent and will need a one-off approval for an exception to our standard renewal terms.

Next steps I propose:
1) Marissa (deal desk) to draft a commercial terms sheet with the proposed cap language (+5% year 3, cumulative not to exceed +8%).
2) Laura (GC liaison) to review the renewal-protection wording and flag any redlines.
3) I'll loop Sean from Sales Ops for capacity commit guidance and to confirm dedicated pool availability for the committed throughput.

We can target internal approvals this week so you can bring to your board June 25. If approved I will escalate to the exec desk for an approval memo.

Thanks,
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference

----
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 09:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Aditya / Laura / Caroline —

Draft commercial term sheet attached. Key commercial points I included:
- Term: 36 months with price lock on months 1–24.
- Uplift: Year 3 uplift capped at +5% relative to year 2. Aggregate uplift across term not to exceed +8%.
- Renewal-protection: We grant a one-time right to match unit economics for a 12-month renewal if change-of-control or region migration occurs, provided contract is renewed within 90 days of the triggering event and committed throughput is at least 70% of prior year.
- Early-termination: standard pro-rata termination fee applies for early cancellation during committed term.

Notes / ask from legal: the renewal-protection clause contains a subjective \"change-of-control\" definition and a throughput threshold — Laura, please review and advise if we should tighten.

Attachment: proposed_terms_redline_v2.docx

Marissa
Sr. Deal Desk Manager, Redwood Inference

----
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 14:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Team —

I've flagged three legal points in the attached redline:
1) Define \"change-of-control\" narrowly to include 50%+ transfer of voting interest or acquisition of substantially all assets.
2) Clarify what constitutes \"region migration\" (majority of traffic migrating regions vs. single-project migration).
3) Add a clause requiring NewCo to provide audited usage reports within 60 days of a renewal request to validate throughput eligibility.

If we agree to those amendments, I recommend an executive pricing exception for the renewal-protection right — it creates a business-risk vector if we are forced to match economics without being able to validate usage. I will prepare a short memo for the exec desk describing legal exposure and recommended guardrails.

Laura Bennett
Deputy General Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: legal_redline_terms.docx

----

title: May invoice: unexpected duplicated token charges — request for investigation
preview:
From: Lina Park <lina.park@sprucetech.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: SpruceTech Accounting <accounting@sprucetech.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: May invoice discrepancy: duplicated token charges?

Hi Dylan,

Our finance team received the May invoice (attached) and we're seeing a cluster of duplicated token charges across two days (May 12–13). The attached redwood_usage_export_may26.csv shows identical session IDs and token counts for repeated POSTs from our ingestion pipeline — looks like a retry/dedup issue on our side, but we want to confirm whether Redwood billed both requests or deduplicated server-side.

Specifically:
- Two session IDs (sess_9c1f and sess_9c1f-dup) show identical token counts (43,210 tokens) and both appear on the invoice.
- Invoice INV-2026-05-789 line 34 and 35 show the duplicate line items.

I've attached the invoice and a short CSV extract. Can your team confirm whether these were billed twice on Redwood's end, and if so, how to proceed with a correction/credit? Our quarter-end VAT reporting is coming up so we need an amended invoice if a credit is issued.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call. I'm available Thu morning.

Best,
Lina Park
Head of Cloud Operations, SpruceTech
Lina.Park@sprucetech.com
Attachments: sprucetech_invoices_May2026.pdf (pdf), redwood_usage_export_may26.csv (csv)

2) If we find double billing due to a platform-side duplication, we'll propose a provisional credit and issue an amended invoice for VAT purposes.
3) If the duplication originated entirely from SpruceTech retries, we'll still help with the investigation and provide the evidence you need for your downstream reconciliation.

Can you confirm the session IDs you mentioned match the identifiers in the CSV (sess_9c1f and sess_9c1f-dup)? Also, if you can send any internal retry logs or the ingestion request IDs that would speed this up. I'm available Thu 9:00–11:30 PT for a call.

I'll treat this as high priority.

—Dylan

Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 4 Jun 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: May invoice discrepancy: duplicated token charges? — initial findings

Hi Lina / Dylan,

I've pulled the billing reconciliation for the window you flagged (May 12 14:00–14:10 UTC). We see two adjacent request records for the same conversation hash and identical token counts — both were matched to charge events in our metering pipeline. At first glance it looks like the requests were not collapsed server-side because the request IDs differed; our de-duplication logic uses a request UUID plus a short time window and, in this case, the upstream retry changed the UUID.

What we can offer immediately: a provisional credit covering the duplicated token volume (43,210 tokens x 2 = 86,420 tokens duplicated) translated to the billed amount on INV-2026-05-789 line items. Pending a full audit, we propose applying the provisional credit to your July invoice and then issuing an amended May invoice showing the VAT correction.

Action items I need from SpruceTech to finalize:
- Confirm whether you want a provisional credit (applied to next invoice) or an immediate refund.
- Confirm the billing contact for the amended invoice (should be accounting@sprucetech.com).

I'll start a deeper audit of the token ingestion logs and share a reconciliation CSV when available.

title: Unexpected surge of 429/5xx — token validation being misreported
preview:
From: Sarah Brown <sarah.brown@brightcart.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@brightcart.com
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: sudden surge of 429/5xx from redwood (auth path)

Hi Evelyn,

We're seeing a sharp increase in 429s and a cluster of 5xx responses across our inference calls to the Redwood hosted API starting ~09:05 PDT. Error rate jumped from ~0.3% to ~7% and our retry logic is kicking off a retry storm which is making things worse.

Key details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate, /v1/chat (region: us-west-2)
- First observed: 2026-06-05T09:05:12-07:00
- Symptoms: 429 + 502 returned frequently; our SREs see auth failures in the logs but the responses are classified as 5xx/429 rather than 401.
- We attached a short summary and some sample headers/log lines.

Attachments: logs_summary.csv (sample counts), jwt-debug.txt (one example token dump)

We need an immediate confirmation that you see this and an ETA for mitigation — our product is heavily impacted.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sr. SRE, BrightCart
[Attachment: logs_summary.csv (CSV, 52KB)] [Attachment: jwt-debug.txt (TXT, 4KB)]

---

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Brown <sarah.brown@brightcart.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 09:37:21 -0700
Subject: Re: sudden surge of 429/5xx from redwood (auth path)

Thanks for the heads up, Sarah — I can see the alerts in our dashboard. I'm escalating to infra and auth triage now.

Immediate steps we are taking:
- Rishi is looking at gateway logs and token validation traces.
- Monica is running a sampling job on requests from BrightCart to reproduce the classification.
- We'll share an interim update within 45 minutes.

Can you confirm whether you rotated any API keys / JWT issuers in the last 24 hours? Also please include a sample X-Request-ID from a 5xx response — that'll help us trace.

Evelyn
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

---

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 10:05:42 -0700
Subject: RE: sudden surge of 429/5xx from redwood (auth path)

Quick triage notes:
- Gateway (auth-proxy) logs show a rise in token validation errors that are logged as internal server errors because an upstream JST validator returned a 502 for a subset of tokens. The proxy layer is mapping some of those upstream validation errors to 5xx instead of 401/403.
- Hypothesis A: a transient partial outage of our key-service (token-issuer introspection) caused downstream calls to return 502 and the proxy misclassified.
- Hypothesis B: clock skew on BrightCart's JWTs caused our validation library to throw unexpected exceptions in the validator, which are bubbling as internal errors.

Repro plan: capture raw request headers and validator traces for ~500 requests and replay in staging with the same validator binary.

I'll attach two grep snippets and point to the trace run once complete.

Rishi
SRE, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: gateway-grep-snippets.txt (TXT, 8KB)]

---

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brown <sarah.brown@brightcart.com>
Cc: ops@brightcart.com
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 14:22:03 -0700
Subject: Findings: misclassification + clock skew pattern

Update from the deeper analysis — TL;DR: two separate but compounding issues.

1) Misclassification path
- When our validator service (key-introspect) returned intermittent 502s, the gateway did not have a defensive mapping and treated the upstream failure as a generic internal error; the client-facing status became 502/5xx.
- That triggered BrightCart to retry; retries were not rate-limited tightly enough and amplified load (retry storm).

2) Clock skew and token validation edge cases
- A portion (~18%) of failing tokens have 'nbf' or 'iat' timestamps that are 1-2 minutes ahead of our node clocks (BrightCart reported a recent auto-scaler image that had NTP misconfiguration). Our validation library raised an exception path in that code path which in some circumstances bubbles up and triggers a 500 from the validator.

Mitigation applied (live):
- Added defensive mapping in the gateway: upstream 502 from validator -> return 401 with a specific X-Auth-Failure header for a subset of known errors (patched at 13:10 PDT).
- Throttled client retries at edge for the BrightCart self-service tokens for 30m window.
- Implemented a short-lived fallback: when key-introspect is 502, we now return 401 instead of 5xx for token failures while preserving trace IDs.

Remaining work / ETA:
- Full hotfix to validator to handle clock skew exceptions: PR in review, ETA 4 hours (target merge 18:30 PDT).
- Postmortem and long-term: add better NTP checks in our onboarding checklist + stricter retry guidance in our SDKs.

Attachments: diag-screenshot.png (trace snapshot)
Related ticket: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/SUP-1234

Monica
Platform Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference

---

From: Sarah Brown <sarah.brown@brightcart.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 7 Jun 2026 08:30:50 -0700
Subject: Re: sudden surge of 429/5xx from redwood (auth path)

Thanks for the fast turnaround. We appreciate the mitigations. Two asks from our side:
1) Can you confirm whether BrightCart will receive a service credit for the downtime window (09:05-13:10 PDT)?
2) We need a short summary we can share with our execs by EOD — root cause + immediate mitigation + ETA for permanent fix.

Our legal team has flagged this as a priority; I copied our SRE alias again.

Sarah
Sr. SRE, BrightCart

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brown <sarah.brown@brightcart.com>
Date: Sun, 7 Jun 2026 17:04:12 -0700
Subject: Re: sudden surge of 429/5xx from redwood (auth path)

Thanks team and thanks Sarah for escalating. A few executive notes and decisions: 

- Good work on the defensive gateway change — that should stop the misclassification and blunt the retry storm.
- Offer: Redwood will issue a pro-rated service credit for the impacted window (we'll calculate credits by token-invoiced minutes and send a proposal by EOD).
- Immediate operational ask: patch the validator to handle clock-skew exceptions and get the PR merged in the next 4 hours. Rishi, please take lead and call any additional hands you need.
- Communication: Evelyn, please prepare the one-paragraph executive summary for Sarah's execs and include the three bullet points: root cause, what we did, and follow-up actions + ETA. Include the SUP-1234 link.

Also: action item for product — update the onboarding checklist to add an NTP validation step and document SDK retry defaults. Monica, add to the postmortem.

Jordan
Co-founder & CEO
Redwood Inference

title: Weekly MAP technical standup: SDK/API/auth/toolchain sync
preview:
From: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff and weekly technical checkpoint

Karthik —

Thanks for the call yesterday. Per our MAP, we want to kick off a short weekly technical checkpoint focused on the integration workstream (SDK + API auth + streaming + tool-calling). Attached is our internal MAP (placeholder) and a short list of evaluation scenarios we will run during the POC.

Top priorities for this week:
- SDK integration: sample app runs end-to-end with tokenized streaming responses (happy path)
- Auth: mutual TLS + token exchange — sample config to be validated against Integracom infra
- Tool calling: validate the invocation flow for external tool endpoints (idempotency, retries)
- Smoke tests and simple throughput targets for 512-token responses

Can we set a recurring 30-min check on Mondays? For this first sync, we want owners and a short list of deliverables for next Tuesday (6/2).

Attachments: MAP_Integracom_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

Samir
Samir Gupta
Technical Program Manager, Integracom

---
On Fri, 28 May 2026 at 09:15 Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com> wrote:
> Karthik — quick note to get us started on the MAP items for SDK and auth.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff and weekly technical checkpoint

Samir — thanks, this is great. Confirming weekly 30-min recurring on Mondays at 10:00 PT starting 2026-06-02. I've sketched owners below for the first checkpoint and some immediate asks so we have focused workstreams by EOD Monday.

Owners & first deliverables (target: 2026-06-02 EOD unless noted):
- SDK integration (owner: Marissa Cole): integrate redwood-js SDK into Integracom sample app; basic generate + streaming tests. Deliverable: sdk-integration-checklist.md + demo video link.
- Auth config (owner: Rishi Malhotra): validate mTLS setup and token exchange flow; provide example auth-sample-config.yaml and test script. Deliverable: auth-sample-config.yaml + test results.
- Tool calling & idempotency (owner: Sean Gallagher): share recommended retry/backoff and signature headers; prototype call to a mock tool endpoint. Deliverable: tool-calling-prototype + notes.
- Evaluation plan & pass/fail (owner: Monica Patel): define 5 acceptance scenarios for the MAP evaluation (latency, correctness, streaming completeness, auth success rate, tool-call idempotency). Deliverable: short acceptance table in MAP_Integracom_v1.xlsx.

Blockers: Integracom needs firewall rules for our test runner IPs — Samir, can you confirm ops will open ports for the demo cluster?

I'll run the calendar invite and include the MAP as a living document.

Thanks — see you Monday.

-Karthik
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

> On Fri, May 28, 2026 at 09:15 AM Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com> wrote:
> > Karthik — quick note to get us started on the MAP items for SDK and auth.


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff and weekly technical checkpoint — SDK status

Quick update from the SDK side ahead of tomorrow's check-in. TL;DR: progress, small gotcha on streaming framing.

What we completed (today):
- Integrated redwood-js 0.9.2 into Integracom sample app; generation API works for non-streaming calls.
- Implemented client-side continuous streaming handler (sends chunked tokens to UI).
- Added simple CI job to run the sample app against our hosted sandbox.

Gotcha discovered:
- Integracom's gateway truncates chunked transfer encoding when an intermediary proxy is present. Streaming tokens are batched into larger frames which increases perceived latency. Workaround options: 1) open proxy config for SSE/chunked, 2) fallback to short-polling for now.

Asks / blockers for the MAP:
- Samir: can Integracom ops allow chunked transfer for the demo runner IPs? If not, we'll include a short-polling fallback in the SDK checklist.
- Rishi: once we confirm auth handshake timings, we'll measure end-to-end latency for streaming vs non-streaming.

Attachments: sdk-integration-checklist.md (text/markdown)

I'll bring a demo snippet to tomorrow's call.

-Marissa
Staff Engineer, Developer Experience

> On Fri, May 28, 2026 at 11:02 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Owners & first deliverables (target: 2026-06-02 EOD unless noted):


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@integracom.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff and weekly technical checkpoint — final pre-checkpoint sync

All — quick wrap before the 10:00 PT checkpoint. Status and next actions: 

Status summary:
- SDK (Marissa): sample app + CI in place, streaming framing work around noted.
- Auth (Rishi): mTLS handshake verified in our lab; sample config attached. One caveat: Integracom's token issuer uses 15s clock skew; we've added a jitter in the exchange flow to tolerate that.
- Tool calling (Sean): prototype endpoint up at https://mock.tools.integracom.dev/call — basic retry + idempotency token implemented. Logs show idempotent responses for retries.
- Evaluation (Monica): acceptance scenarios updated in MAP_Integracom_v1.xlsx; added pass criteria for streaming completeness.

Action items (owner — due):
- Marissa — add fallback path to SDK checklist (short-polling) and document detection heuristic (due 2026-06-04).
- Rishi — share auth-sample-config.yaml and a short runbook for token exchange including skew handling (done, attached).
- Sean — enable a signed-header flow on the mock tool endpoint and share curl examples (due 2026-06-04).
- Samir / Integracom Ops — confirm firewall/proxy change for demo runner IPs; if not feasible, confirm acceptance of short-poll fallback (due 2026-06-03).
- Monica — run the first evaluation scenario (basic correctness + streaming) against the sandbox and report (due 2026-06-05).

Blockers: awaiting Integracom ops confirmation on proxy behavior (Samir). If proxy changes are blocked, we will proceed with short-polling fallback for the demo and mark streaming performance as "degraded" in MAP.

Next call: recurring Mondays 10:00 PT — first standing check tomorrow 2026-06-02 10:00 PT. I'll capture meeting notes in the MAP spreadsheet after the call.

Attachments: auth-sample-config.yaml (text/x-yaml)

Thanks — Rishi
Principal Engineer, Inference Runtime

preview:
From: Tom Hsu <billing@acme-data.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Wong <claire@acme-data.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:17:00 -0700
Subject: Failed card on renewal attempt — invoice 05/2026

Hi Jasmine,

We received the renewal notice but the card on file declined when Stripe attempted to process invoice INV-05-2026 for $18,500. Can you confirm what happened and whether you can re-attempt the charge? Our procurement team needs the payment captured before the end of the month to avoid service interruption.

Also, Claire mentioned we might need an updated invoice with VAT/US tax lines for our internal accounting — is that possible?

Thanks,
Tom Hsu
Head of Finance
Acme Data Services
attachments: invoice_05-2026.pdf (PDF)


From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
To: Tom Hsu <billing@acme-data.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Claire Wong <claire@acme-data.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Failed card on renewal attempt — invoice 05/2026

Hi Tom — thanks for flagging this. I see a single decline on 2026-05-20 from Stripe with code card_declined. We can do two things immediately:

- Schedule a single retry today at a lower retry window to minimize risk of automatic retries from Stripe that sometimes trigger additional declines.
- If you'd prefer to update the card, we can send a secure card update link that expires in 24 hours.

For VAT/tax lines: we can issue a corrected invoice with a separate tax line and include your VAT number if you provide it. Would you like the retry first, or should we wait for a card update and reissue the invoice with VAT?

I'll loop in Aditya to coordinate the retry and attach the current retry log once we run it.

Best,
Jasmine Liu
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jasmine@redwood.com] | +1 (415) 555-0193
Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Tom Hsu <billing@acme-data.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Claire Wong <claire@acme-data.com>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Failed card on renewal attempt — retry & evidence

Tom — quick update from billing ops: we scheduled a manual retry for 2026-05-21 09:30 PT (one attempt only). I attached the retry attempt log and a copy of the original invoice. If that attempt fails, our standard next step is to send the secure card update link to the billing contact.

A few notes to avoid an unnecessary dispute/chargeback:
- If you update the card, please complete the update within 24 hours so we can capture before your internal cutoff.
- If the cardholder does dispute the charge with their bank, please share any receipts or SOW that show acceptance of services — we'll bundle evidence for Stripe (usage logs, signed SOW).
- We can also pause automatic renewals for a short window if you need to coordinate internally.

Attaching: invoice_2026-05-01.pdf, retry_attempt_log.txt

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Billing Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Tom Hsu <billing@acme-data.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Wong <claire@acme-data.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2026 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Failed card on renewal attempt — retry & evidence

Hi Aditya / Jasmine — thanks for the retry log. The retry failed again with the same decline code. Claire and I will update the payment method tonight and Claire will confirm once done. We also need the VAT line added to the invoice before we close the PO.

One other thing: our finance team saw a notification from Stripe about a potential dispute window opening for the previous cycle (not this attempted renewal). Can Redwood prepare a short evidence bundle we can upload to Stripe if required? Ideally: signed SOW, invoice, delivery logs for April/May, and the account contact confirmation.

title: rw-13b latency surge and timeouts — customer impact & mitigation
preview:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: ops@quanttrade.io
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 08:12:00 -0000
Subject: rw-13b latency surge and timeouts — interim mitigation

Hi Emma / QuantTrade ops team,

We’re reaching out to let you know we’re observing an elevated rate of latency spikes and timeouts for the rw-13b-short model in the us-west-2 region starting ~2026-05-21 07:40 UTC. Some requests are returning 502/504-style failures or timing out after our normal client timeout window. Impacted requests are predominantly those with long prompt prefixes (>800 tokens) and open-loop sampling.

What we’re doing now (interim):
- Routing new requests away from the affected slice to rw-13b-quantized where possible.
- Applying a temporary conservative batch size cap to reduce KV cache pressure.
- Enabling request-level priority fallback: short-path responses served from cached prefixes.

Expected effect: you should see a reduction in timeouts for new requests in the next 15–30 minutes. We will monitor and follow up with a recovery update.

Attachments: query-snippet-20260521.txt (example request that triggered timeouts)

If you see continued failures, please reply with a sample request id and timestamp and we’ll prioritize it. Apologies for the disruption — Sean (on-call, Inference Reliability)

--
Sean Gallagher
Sr. SRE, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.com


From: Emma Li <emma.li@quanttrade.io>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@quanttrade.io, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 08:29:14 -0000
Subject: Re: rw-13b latency surge and timeouts — interim mitigation

Thanks Sean — we started seeing errors around 07:45 UTC and it impacted our realtime quoting workflow (multiple requests per second). Affected flows are: new-quote-generator, risk-scan (both use the rw-13b-short preset).

We appreciate the routing to rw-13b-quantized. Two quick asks:
1) Can you confirm whether logs will include the request ids so we can map failures to our internal traces?
2) If the conservative batching reduces token throughput, can you estimate impact on per-call latency?

We’ll forward a concrete request id in a follow-up if we see it again.

— Emma
Emma Li
Head of Product, QuantTrade

- Confirm root cause hypothesis (KV cache eviction pressure vs. a problematic kernel on the affected instance type)
- Share ETA for either a full rollback to a stable kernel/hotfix or confirmation that the routing mitigation is holding
- Prepare a one-paragraph customer-visible update that we can send in 30–60 minutes

I’m tagging Monica for SLA/credits guidance if we need to apply an exception.

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: ops@quanttrade.io
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 23 May 2026 16:45:00 -0000
Subject: Incident wrap-up and next steps — rw-13b latency surge (postmortem draft)

Hi Emma / QuantTrade team,

Thank you for your patience while we triaged the rw-13b latency/timeouts incident that began on 2026-05-21 07:40 UTC. Below is a concise post-incident summary and the actions we’ve taken so far. Please treat this as a near-final draft; we’re happy to add any details you need for your internal audit.

Summary:
- Impact: Elevated request timeouts and 5xx-like failures observed for rw-13b-short in us-west-2 between 2026-05-21 07:40 UTC and 2026-05-21 10:15 UTC, with residual elevated latency for specific long-prefix workloads until 2026-05-23 15:30 UTC. Estimated affected request volume for QuantTrade: ~0.6% of calls during the window; error rate peaked at ~6% for long-prefix requests.
- Root cause (short): A pathological interaction between a new kernel memory allocator tweak and our KV cache eviction policy on a subset of instances, which increased latency variance under sustained long-prefix workloads. The allocator change was rolled out in a routine driver update and exposed a worst-case under heavy KV churn.
- Immediate mitigation: We routed traffic to rw-13b-quantized, applied a conservative batch cap, and enabled prefix-based short-path fallback. These reduced visible errors by ~85% within 30 minutes.
- Permanent remediation planned:
  1) Roll back the allocator tweak on the affected instance family and re-run canary tests (completed 2026-05-22 23:10 UTC).
  2) Implement a heap-pressure detector in the runtime to throttle batch sizes proactively when KV churn is detected (ETA: 2026-06-07).
  3) Add a guided configuration for customers to set a recommended prefix-cutoff when using rw-13b for realtime flows (documentation + SDK warning; ETA: 2026-06-14).

Compensation/SLA: Monica Patel (Customer Success) will reach out separately regarding any SLA credit eligibility.

Attachments: postmortem-draft-20260523.pdf, incident_runtime_logs.zip

Action items (owner / ETA):
- Marcus Lin — Complete kernel rollback validation and publish canary report (done: 2026-05-22 23:10 UTC).
- Markus Klein — Ship runtime heap-pressure detector spec (drafted; due 2026-05-28).
- Sean Gallagher — Add KB entry with recommended temporary client-side prefix cutoff and example settings (ETA: 2026-05-24).
- Monica Patel — Contact QuantTrade on SLA credits and next steps (ETA: 2026-05-24).

If you want any additional logs, specific request-id mappings, or an internal runbook for your SRE team, let us know and we’ll supply them. Thanks again — Hannah

--
Hannah Schmitt
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference
hannah.schmitt@redwood.com
